title: App white-screens on launch — dependabot PR #252 bumped react without react-dom
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED CRITICAL REGRESSION (2026-06-02).

Symptom: launching tuxlink (tauri dev OR production) shows a white screen with no content. React refuses to mount.

Root cause: PR #252 (dependabot) bumped 'react' from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 + '@types/react' from 19.2.15 to 19.2.16, but did NOT bump 'react-dom' (still at 19.2.6). React 19 enforces strict version parity between 'react' and 'react-dom' — peer mismatch throws an uncaught runtime exception at mount time:

  Error: Incompatible React versions: The 'react' and 'react-dom' packages must have the exact same version. Instead got:
    - react:      19.2.7
    - react-dom:  19.2.6
  Learn more: https://react.dev/warnings/version-mismatch

Captured via headless chromium + CDP against http://localhost:1420 of the converged build.

Fix:

1. Bump react-dom from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 in package.json
2. Bump @types/react-dom to match react-dom (current spec says ^18.3.0 — this is also stale; should be ^19.2.x)
3. Run 'pnpm install' to regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml
4. Verify: 'pnpm exec tsc --noEmit' clean + 'pnpm vitest run' clean
5. Verify: headless chromium loads localhost:1420 and #root has content after mount

Follow-up hardening (defer to P2 bd):
- Configure dependabot's 'groups' so react + react-dom + @types/react + @types/react-dom always bump together. This SHOULD prevent the same class of bug from recurring. Edit .github/dependabot.yml to add a 'react-monorepo' group:
    groups:
      react:
        patterns:
          - 'react'
          - 'react-dom'
          - '@types/react'
          - '@types/react-dom'

Discovered: 2026-06-02 operator-smoked the converged build after merge of #252 / release 0.17.0.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-ola6-react-dom-version-sync. Branch: bd-tuxlink-ola6/react-dom-version-sync. Created 2026-06-02 21:53:21 UTC by plover-willow-basalt.

title: Position UI/on-air locator conflation — ribbon shows config_grid not live GPS fix under LocalUiOnly
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED REGRESSION (post tuxlink-c79g merge).

Symptom: with config { position_source: Gps, gps_state: LocalUiOnly, identity.grid: 'DM33', position_precision: SixCharGrid } and a fresh GPS fix (verified: gpsd producing TPV mode=3 at ~1Hz, tuxlink connected, arbiter.has_fresh_fix()=true), the ribbon displays 'DM33' instead of the live precision-reduced fix grid 'DM33ww'.

Root cause: effective_broadcast_locator in src-tauri/src/position/mod.rs:18-49 conflates two distinct concerns:
  (a) ribbon DISPLAY = what to show the operator locally
  (b) on-air LOCATOR = what gets transmitted (privacy-gated by gps_state)

The Codex P1-B finding from the prior position-subsystem-restoration 5-round adrev (PR #215 / tuxlink-c79g) intentionally collapsed (a)+(b) into one helper, with rustdoc 'the ribbon always shows exactly what is/would be transmitted.' That collapse is the design error: under LocalUiOnly + source=Gps + fresh fix, the operator's literal intent ('see GPS locally, don't broadcast') is broken because the local display falls back to config_grid.

Fix (corrected Path A, validated by Codex consultation 2026-06-01):

Backend:
  - position/mod.rs: add effective_ui_locator(config, arbiter):
      source=Gps + fresh fix + gps_state in {LocalUiOnly, BroadcastAtPrecision} → arbiter.broadcast_grid() (live fix at precision)
      source=Gps + (no fresh fix OR gps_state=Off) → config_grid fallback
      source=Manual → config_grid
  - Keep effective_broadcast_locator unchanged (on-air gating preserved).
  - Update rustdoc on broadcast helper: it is single source of truth for on-air ONLY.
  - ui_commands.rs PositionStatusDto: add ui_grid: String. Populate via effective_ui_locator.

Frontend:
  - useStatus.ts: add ui_grid to PositionStatusDto type. liveGrid reads ui_grid (not broadcast_grid).
  - GridEdit.tsx: clean stale pjih-era comments.

Spec amendment (SAME PR, first commit per propagation contract):
  - docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-position-subsystem-restoration-design.md
  - Amend vocabulary, §2.5, §3.1, §3.2, §3.4, §6.1, §6.4
  - Replace 'ribbon equals on-air locator' with 'ribbon equals local display locator; on-air locator remains separately privacy-gated'
  - Codex P1-B finding gets a corrective note (5-round adrev correctly identified ambiguity but proposed the wrong fix shape)

Tests (TDD):
  - Backend matrix for effective_ui_locator: 5 spec states × {Off, LocalUiOnly, BroadcastAtPrecision}
  - Regression: source=Gps + LocalUiOnly + fresh fix + SixCharGrid → ui_grid='DM33ww' AND broadcast_grid='DM33' (intentionally differ)
  - DTO serialization test: both ui_grid and broadcast_grid present + serialize snake_case
  - Hook test: ribbon liveGrid reads ui_grid
  - Existing Codex P1-B tests: rename/reframe (don't blind-delete coverage)

Adjacent bug Codex flagged (decide in PR or defer):
  - gps_ready is arbiter.has_fresh_fix() regardless of gps_state. If operator switches state to Off mid-session, gpsd client task isn't killed → fixes keep arriving → gps_ready stays true. Should gate gps_ready false under Off.

Codex full consultation transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-01-position-localuionly-display-conflation-codex.md (4468 lines, gitignored, kept on disk for reference).

Base off current origin/main (NOT bd-tuxlink-c79g worktree which is now stale; PRs #216-#219 landed after #215).

title: REGRESSION fix(position): chip stuck GPS-green + accepts manual input but doesn't switch — pjih over-removed operator agency
description:
Operator-reported 2026-06-01 post-PR #189 merge: 'GPS is now fully broken. It doesn't switch to manual entry with [the prior] gps available [affordance], it just says GPS green at all times and accepts/displays whatever input. That's major regression.'

ROOT CAUSE (suspected — needs investigation)
PR #189 (tuxlink-pjih) decoupled set_manual from source-pinning and removed the explicit Use-GPS button from GridEdit, on the assumption that effective_source derived live from arbiter state would be sufficient signal. That over-removed operator agency:
  - The chip now reads positionStatus.active_source (derived: Gps when fresh fix, Manual otherwise).
  - With gpsd publishing fixes, effective_source=Gps permanently → chip is permanently GPS-green.
  - The operator can no longer FORCE Manual mode for an explicit on-air override.
  - Typing in the grid updates config.identity.grid + arbiter.manual_grid; under gps_state != BroadcastAtPrecision the broadcast falls back to config.identity.grid, so the display 'accepts whatever input' even though chip says GPS.

REQUIRED FIX (shape — operator decides)
Restore explicit operator agency over source selection. Options:
  (a) Restore the use-gps button + reintroduce stored source as the chip's primary signal (closest to pre-pjih, but pjih's stuck-in-Manual bug returns).
  (b) Add an explicit Manual ↔ GPS toggle separate from grid-edit; chip shows operator's choice; arbiter respects it.
  (c) Hybrid: chip shows effective source by default; affordance to override (force Manual / force GPS) lives in the source chip itself or in Settings.

SEVERITY P0 — operator-visible major regression caused by my PR #189.

title: RADIO-1: packet connect is unsafe on-air — unbounded airtime, abort can't halt TX, pre-connect DISC
description: INCIDENT 2026-05-22: a packet dial over the new RFCOMM socket ran ~110s of repeated SABM keying with no reachable abort; operator had to power OFF the radio to stop it. This is a RADIO-1 violation. Do NOT dial packet (any transport) until ALL of the below land. Found via Codex adrev (dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-socket-rx-runaway-codex.md, gitignored). FIX BUNDLE: (1) Bounded airtime — split the connect SABM-retry policy from data N2; one SABM + at most one retry + a HARD total-elapsed cap (~20-30s) regardless of n2_retries. (2) Abort before every transmit — AbortableByteLink::write must check the flag (currently only read() does, link.rs:80); connect() must check abort before push_kiss_params and before EACH SABM (currently TXes at top of each attempt); never key after Cancel. (3) No pre-connect DISC — connect() builds Ax25Stream{closed:false} before the link is up, so a failed dial (DM/timeout/abort) drops the stream -> Drop -> disconnect() -> sends DISC + waits T1 (datalink.rs:714). Only arm teardown after a UA is received. (4) REVERT the T1 floor (config.rs into_params MIN_RF_T1_MS, added this session) — it tripled worst-case airtime (3s->10s per retransmit) and was the wrong lever. (5) Build/test on task-amd-main-ui (has the abort + resize UI); main lacks them. Blocks tuxlink-uhc and tuxlink-nx2.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: Hotfix: right-click MessageContextMenu (Move to folder)
description: Phase 2 MVP (PR #284) deferred the right-click message context menu to Phase 3, but spec §6 D5 explicitly promised it as the v1 move path. Operator can't move messages by the natural method. Hotfix: right-click on a message row -> context menu with Open / Reply / Reply All / Forward / Archive / Move to (submenu of system + user folders). Click Move to -> folder fires the existing moveOpen handler. The reading-pane Move dropdown was the only path in MVP -- not discoverable. Drag-drop stays deferred to Phase 3.

title: Update CMS-request grounding doc with WLE-install empirical findings + file sprint sub-issues
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-v8ee (PR #282 merged). Operator pointed at the WLE reference install at dev/scratch/winlink-re/install/RMS Express/. Source confirms:

1. Catalog inquiry mechanism (literal wire format from N7CPZ outbox):
   - To: INQUIRY@winlink.org
   - Subject: REQUEST
   - Body: multipart/mixed > text/plain, one inquiry filename per line
   - Multiple inquiries per message supported (verified: PUB_PACKET + PUB_VARA in one msg)

2. Catalog database exists at N7CPZ/Data/Winlink Queries.txt — 1477 entries across 127 categories. Pipe-delimited: CATEGORY|FILENAME|DESCRIPTION|SIZE. Includes WL2K_RMS/PUB_<MODE> entries (PACKET, VARA, ARDOP, PACTOR, ROBUST).

3. RMS station list (Q2 answer): WLE legacy IS in-band via the catalog system. PUB_PACKET / PUB_VARA / etc. are catalog inquiries. HTTPS api.winlink.org is Pat-specific, NOT what WLE uses. So 'match WLE' = in-band only; HTTPS-only or both-with-fallback are optional adds.

4. GRIB (Q3 elaboration): WLE catalog contains ZERO grib filenames — only WL2K_HELP/CUSTOM.GRIB and WL2K_HELP/MAXSAEA_GRIB which are help docs about Saildocs. So WLE's GRIB-file-request menu composes Saildocs message; the CMS has no GRIB. Rules out Q3 option (b).

This task:
- Update docs/design/2026-06-02-cms-request-protocol-grounding.md with the empirical findings section + Q1/Q2 RESOLVED + Q3 narrowed
- File per-feature sub-issues for the sprint:
  A. Catalog inquiry framework (bundle Winlink Queries.txt, tree picker UI, send-as-REQUEST-to-INQUIRY composition, response renderer)
  B. GRIB request via Saildocs (parameter form, send to query@saildocs.com, save attachment)
  C. (optional, operator's choice) RMS list via HTTPS fast-path (Pat-style /gateway/status.json)
- Bulletins fold into A (they're catalog inquiries in ARES_RACES / NEWS / etc. categories)
- RMS station list folds into A for the legacy/in-band path; only C is separate
- Architecture is much smaller than originally framed in tuxlink-2u4n
notes: PR #283 open. Doc update with empirical findings from WLE install. Q1+Q2 fully resolved, Q3 narrowed to B1/B2 choice. Sprint architecture collapses to 2 sub-issues (catalog framework + GRIB-Saildocs).

title: [P0] Ground WLE CMS-request protocol via Pat source clone (no code yet)
description:
Blocks all of the tuxlink-2u4n sprint. Per project's AI-on-Winlink reliability discipline, the request vocabulary, addressing, and response shapes must be sourced from a canonical implementation, not agent memory.

Task:
1. git clone https://github.com/la5nta/pat /tmp/pat-reference (read-only reference; will not be vendored)
2. Identify request implementations: catalog, station list, bulletins, GRIB. Likely under cmd/pat/ or internal/
3. Capture: addressing (REQUEST@ vs service@ vs custom), body templates, response parsing assumptions, attachment handling
4. Cross-reference with any spec docs in docs/ (Winlink B2F notes if any)
5. Write dev/scratch/2026-06-02-cms-request-protocol-grounding.md (gitignored; per-machine reference) capturing the findings. Include the EXACT request body strings for each type, the destination address vocabulary, and the response format.
6. File a per-request-type bd issue (catalog, stations, bulletins, GRIB) — each with the concrete protocol details from step 5. Each will be the spec the implementation PRs work from.

NO RUST/TS CODE THIS ISSUE. This is purely research. /tmp/pat-reference is a throwaway clone; we don't vendor or import Pat code per project_pat_complete_strip_directive_2026_05_30.

Output: 4 new bd issues (one per request type) ready to claim, plus the grounding doc at dev/scratch/. Sprint blocker tuxlink-2u4n unblocks once these exist.
notes: PR #282 open with grounding doc at docs/design/2026-06-02-cms-request-protocol-grounding.md. Three operator decisions surfaced (catalog inquiry list source, RMS list mechanism, GRIB scope). Sprint paused pending operator answers. Loop ended this iteration per protocol-assumption-invalidated stop condition.

title: Tuxlink .desktop files use 'Exec=tuxlink' which GIO rejects in dev (tuxlink not on PATH) — wf-panel-pi can't load entry → default icon
description:
tuxlink-xcay confirmed Wayland app_id is 'tuxlink' and PR #276 installed matching tuxlink.desktop + tuxlink.png. Still showed default icon. Diagnosed via GIO:

  Gio.DesktopAppInfo.new('tuxlink.desktop') → NULL

GIO validates that the Exec command resolves on PATH. In dev mode, the tuxlink binary lives at .local/converge-build-worktree/src-tauri/target/debug/tuxlink (not on PATH). Production .deb installs to /usr/bin/tuxlink (on PATH) — but that path doesn't exist in dev. So 'Exec=tuxlink' fails validation, GIO returns NULL, wf-panel-pi skips the entry, taskbar falls back to default icon.

Confirmed by bisection:
- Exec=tuxlink → NULL
- Exec=/usr/bin/tuxlink → NULL (file doesn't exist in dev)
- Exec=/usr/bin/env tuxlink → OK
- Exec=/bin/sh -c '...' → OK

Fix: change Exec to '/usr/bin/env tuxlink' in both scripts/tuxlink.desktop and scripts/com.tuxlink.app.desktop. /usr/bin/env exists in any POSIX install; env then finds tuxlink on PATH (production) or errors out gracefully (dev — operator launches via pnpm dev:converged anyway).

Discovered: 2026-06-02 after PR #276 still showed default icon. Diagnostic chain:
1. wlrctl toplevel list → confirmed app_id=tuxlink (matches our installed files)
2. GTK icon theme lookup → confirmed tuxlink.png resolves at all sizes
3. Gio.DesktopAppInfo.new('tuxlink.desktop') → NULL (smoking gun)
4. Minimal .desktop with Exec=tuxlink → NULL
5. Same with Exec=/usr/bin/env tuxlink → OK

title: Taskbar icon still not showing after tuxlink-mj7i — Tauri likely sets Wayland app_id to 'tuxlink' (binary name), not 'com.tuxlink.app'
description:
tuxlink-mj7i shipped scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh + canonical com.tuxlink.app.desktop. After install, operator still sees the default icon in wf-panel-pi's taskbar.

Diagnosis:
- All 4 icon sizes installed to ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/<size>/apps/com.tuxlink.app.png ✓
- ~/.local/share/applications/com.tuxlink.app.desktop installed ✓
- desktop-file-validate clean ✓
- BUT: gdbus list-names --session does NOT show com.tuxlink.app registered
- AND: strings on the tuxlink binary doesn't surface com.tuxlink.app as a literal at the obvious places
- Strong evidence Tauri did NOT register GApplication.application_id = com.tuxlink.app
- Wayland app_id therefore likely defaults to the binary name: tuxlink

The .desktop filename + Icon= + StartupWMClass all used com.tuxlink.app, which does NOT match Wayland app_id=tuxlink. wf-panel-pi's window-list module fails to map the running window to my .desktop entry → falls back to default icon.

Fix: install BOTH variants. Copy icons under both com.tuxlink.app.png AND tuxlink.png in hicolor; install both com.tuxlink.app.desktop (current canonical) AND tuxlink.desktop (binary-name variant). Whatever app_id Tauri actually sets, ONE will match.

Cleaner long-term fix: explicitly set the Wayland app_id in Rust at WindowBuilder time via tao's wmclass() / set_app_id() API. Defer to follow-up bd.

For dependent confirmation of the actual app_id: ask operator to install wlrctl (apt install wlrctl) which provides wlrctl toplevel list — enumerates xdg_toplevel.app_id of every running window.

Discovered: 2026-06-02 operator-reported PR #261 didn't fix the icon.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-xcay-taskbar-icon-dual-app-id. Branch: bd-tuxlink-xcay/taskbar-icon-dual-app-id. Created 2026-06-02 22:25:28 UTC by plover-willow-basalt.

title: Tuxlink taskbar shows default Tauri/missing-app icon on Linux — missing .desktop file + XDG icon install
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED UX REGRESSION (2026-06-02).

Symptom: launching tuxlink via tauri dev OR a production .deb install shows the default Tauri/missing-app icon in the taskbar, NOT the Tuxlink-branded icon. The icon appears correctly in the title bar / window decoration (Tauri's WebviewWindow::set_icon() works) but the WM's taskbar lookup fails to find a matching .desktop file.

Root cause: there's no .desktop file installed in ~/.local/share/applications/ (or system equivalent). The window's Wayland app_id is com.tuxlink.app (derived from tauri.conf.json identifier via Tauri's GApplication setup), but no .desktop file exists at that name, so labwc / GNOME / KDE etc. fall back to a generic icon.

Production .deb builds: Tauri's cargo-deb bundler SHOULD generate a .desktop file automatically, but (a) tauri.conf.json bundle.linux config is unset/minimal, (b) StartupWMClass may not be set, (c) operator hasn't tested deb-install path.

Fix:

1. New scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh (idempotent, dev-mode operator runs once):
   - Copies src-tauri/icons/{32x32,128x128,128x128@2x,icon.png} to ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/{32x32,128x128,256x256,512x512}/apps/com.tuxlink.app.png
   - Writes ~/.local/share/applications/com.tuxlink.app.desktop with:
       [Desktop Entry]
       Name=Tuxlink
       GenericName=Winlink HF/VHF/UHF radio client
       Exec=tuxlink
       Icon=com.tuxlink.app
       Type=Application
       Categories=Network;HamRadio;
       StartupWMClass=com.tuxlink.app
       Terminal=false
   - Runs update-desktop-database + gtk-update-icon-cache (best-effort)

2. tauri.conf.json hardening:
   - bundle.category = 'Network' (or hamradio if recognized)
   - bundle.linux.deb config to guarantee .desktop file generation
   - Confirm productName=tuxlink + identifier=com.tuxlink.app stay consistent (they currently do)

3. CONTRIBUTING.md one-line: 'On Linux, run bash scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh once after first clone to register the taskbar icon for tauri dev.'

4. Operator smoke: bash scripts/install-desktop-entry.sh; kill tauri dev; pnpm tauri dev; observe taskbar shows Tuxlink icon (not default).

Discovered: 2026-06-02 operator smoking the converged build.

title: [bug] search: SchemaDrift at startup wedges the SearchService (silent search + indexing failures)
description:
Production: operator reports 'indexing fails with an error stating it failed when attempted, and search silently completely fails without an index backing it.' Root cause: src-tauri/src/search/index.rs detects schema drift on Index::open when an older search.db is on disk (e.g. user_version=1 from pre-tuxlink-g4dj indices). build_service in search/mod.rs propagates this. The setup hook at src-tauri/src/lib.rs:142-145 just eprintln's the failure and continues without app.manage(svc) — so the SearchService is NEVER installed. All search/index Tauri commands then fail because their tauri::State<SearchService> isn't managed. The rebuild_index command would recover, but it's wedged behind the same missing state.

Fix: in build_service (or its setup-hook caller), detect IndexError::SchemaDrift specifically and delete search.db + search.db-wal + search.db-shm before retrying Index::open. The index is regenerable from disk per design (see search/mod.rs comment). After recovery the service installs cleanly; the operator can then click Rebuild Index to repopulate from the mbox source.

Not the same as tuxlink-xoom (TOCTOU on path.exists in Index::open — separate race-condition bug).
notes: PR #262 open. bluff-birch-cove. Fix in build_service catches IndexError::SchemaDrift, deletes search.db + WAL/SHM siblings, re-opens with current schema. 3 new tests in search::module_smoke.

title: [BUG] VARA Start click no-ops on aarch64 — platformBlocked guard left in onStartClick handler
description:
Operator (2026-06-02): PR #237 + #236 merged + visible (banner text confirms), but Start button still does nothing on click. Diagnosis: PR #231 removed platformBlocked from the disabled={...} prop (so button is clickable) but LEFT the same guard in onStartClick's early-return: 'if (busy || platformBlocked) return;'. The button is enabled, the click registers, the handler runs, then early-returns silently. No state change, no error, no visible feedback.

Fix: remove platformBlocked from the onStartClick guard. Keep the banner + the title tooltip (those are informational, correct as-is).

This is the third iteration on the same conceptual bug (PR #231 → #237 → this). Lesson: when removing a gate from one site, audit ALL sites that gate on the same predicate.

title: [BUG] VARA panel inputs permanently disabled — useVaraConfig.loading stuck at true on Pi (real fix for tuxlink-ze98 regression)
description:
Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt): VARA panel inputs + Start button still disabled despite PR #231 (tuxlink-ze98) supposedly removing platformBlocked from the disabled prop.

ROOT CAUSE
useVaraConfig (introduced in PR #221) has a 'loading: boolean' state initialized to true and cleared in the .finally() of config_get_vara's invoke. VaraRadioPanel.tsx wires loading into disabled=={loading || isOpen} on form inputs and disabled={busy || loading || isOpen} on Start. If 'loading' stays true (for any reason — Strict Mode race, Tauri invoke channel issue, JS exception in .then before .finally, etc.), the entire form locks.

CONFIRMED via Cameron's diagnostic: Stop is disabled (so isOpen=false), inputs disabled (so loading=true since isOpen=false), no DevTools errors he can surface.

KEY INSIGHT
usePacketConfig (the pattern PR #221 claimed to mirror) does NOT have a 'loading' state. It only has 'config: PacketConfigDto | null'. Callers check null if they need a loading indicator. My add of 'loading' was over-engineering that introduced a failure mode.

FIX
Mirror usePacketConfig exactly:
1. Remove 'loading' state from useVaraConfig — return only {config, setConfig}
2. Remove 'loading' from disabled prop in VaraRadioPanel — inputs editable from mount
3. Accept the tiny race window: if config_get_vara resolves AFTER operator's first edit, the operator's edit gets overwritten. In practice, vanishingly rare; KISS over robustness here.

ROOT-CAUSE INVESTIGATION
Separate concern — why was loading stuck? Possible reasons:
- React Strict Mode race where both first-mount cleanup and second-mount completion miss the setLoading clear
- Tauri invoke channel hang for config_get_vara on this specific binary
- JS exception in .then before .finally
Worth investigating later as a defensive lesson, but the fix doesn't need to wait for the root cause.

title: Replace source chip toggle with segmented control [GPS|MANUAL] for discoverable source switching
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED UX REGRESSION (post tuxlink-va1i merge).

Symptom: with the position-subsystem-restoration in place, the only path to switch from source=Manual to source=Gps is to click the chip labeled 'MANUAL'. The chip visually reads as a status badge — no human intuits 'click my current state to change it.' Cameron's exact words: 'No human would think to do that.'

Root cause: T12 made the chip DOM-conditional (<button> when Manual, <span role=status> when Gps) with aria-pressed={false} on the Manual button. ARIA-correct for screen readers but the visual design doesn't communicate togglability. T11's '● GPS ready' hint span only renders under source=Manual && gpsReady, so under 'Manual + no fresh fix' there's no visual cue that GPS is even an option.

Fix: replace the single source chip with a segmented control:
  [GPS] [MANUAL]
- Both segments always visible, always clickable.
- Selected segment is filled (highlighted style); other is outlined.
- Clicking unselected segment fires position_set_source('Gps' or 'Manual').
- The '● GPS ready' hint folds INTO the GPS segment as an indicator dot when fresh fix present.

A11y: <div role='radiogroup' aria-label='Position source'> with two <button role='radio' aria-checked={selected}> children. Standard radio-group pattern (NOT toggle-button anymore).

State rendering examples:

title: P2P-Telnet sends FF (no proposals) — outbox not wired into telnet_p2p_connect
description:
Operator smoke 2026-06-02 00:17 UTC against Windows WLE: TCP connect succeeds, telnet-login wrapper completes, B2F handshake exchanges cleanly (;FW: + [tuxlink-0.0.1-B2FHM$] + ; N7CPZ DE N7CPZ (DM25xe)), then tuxlink emits 'FF' (no proposals). WLE responds 'FQ' and closes. Messages sent: 0 / received: 0 — despite the operator having drafts addressed to cameronzucker@gmail.com AND to n7cpz target that should have flowed.

Root cause: src-tauri/src/ui_commands.rs telnet_p2p_connect passes an empty Vec<OutboundMessage> to telnet_p2p::connect_and_exchange. The PR-1 plan Task 4 explicitly flagged this as a stub:

> // PR 1: empty outbox stub. Real outbox query comes when integrating
> // with the draft store (mirror however telnet_dial does it — if it's
> // already in this file, follow that pattern).
> let outbound: Vec<OutboundMessage> = Vec::new();

The fix:

1. Query the native draft/outbox store for outbound messages. The store lives at ~/.local/share/tuxlink/mbox/<callsign>/out/ as .b2f files (per tuxlink-su2h's description).

2. For P2P semantics: send ALL queued outbound messages (operator's WLE acts as their CMS-proxy in the Post Office model — recipient routing is WLE's job). Do NOT filter by peer callsign at the tuxlink dial-time layer; let the receiving station / its CMS uplink handle delivery.

3. Marshal the .b2f files into Vec<OutboundMessage> (the type lives at src-tauri/src/winlink/session.rs:32). Check how cms_connect → backend.connect(TransportConfig::Cms{...}) → WinlinkBackend impl assembles its outbound list. The P2P path bypasses WinlinkBackend so it needs its own outbox-read step.

4. On the receive side: emit_session_line should report each ;FW: line received, each turn taken, and per-message receive counts so the operator sees something happening in the session log when messages flow.

5. The result struct P2pDialResult already has sent_count + received_count — make sure these are populated from the ExchangeResult.

Reference traces from the operator smoke (cleanly handshaked but stub-empty outbox path):

  *** Received incoming connection from 100.110.97.120 at 2026-06-02 00:17:57 UTC
  *** Logon by N7CPZ
     ;FW: N7CPZ
     [RMS Express-1.8.1.0-B2FHM$]
     N7CPZ DE N7CPZ >

  ;FW: N7CPZ
  [tuxlink-0.0.1-B2FHM$]
  ; N7CPZ DE N7CPZ (DM25xe)
  FF
     FQ

Blocked by the related tuxlink-su2h: the operator cannot verify outbox contents from the UI right now, so they can't confirm 'I have N drafts queued that should be flowing.' The outbox-UI fix is independent and should land alongside this.

title: [convergence-discipline] Branch state machine ADR + pre-commit/pre-push hooks
description:
Codex P0 #1 + P2 #17. Defines branch lifecycle states (active / PR-open / merged-dead / follow-up / disposed) and enforces transitions via standard git pre-commit/pre-push hooks (NOT Claude-specific so they work for Codex CLI / human shell). Catches the v1p failure mode (commits to a dead branch after merge) at commit-time not merge-time. The ADR is the canonical source; CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md become pointers per documentation propagation contract.

title: Convergence discipline: branch-state-machine + converge-build script + dev-server lease + audit log
description:
Codified discipline so the operator can periodically surface a single-source-of-truth consolidated build without re-litigating each time. Derived from 2026-06-01 morning forensic session where 'orphan post-merge commits' (v1p), 858-commit-stale operator branch, pnpm symlink-stale, .beads/issues.jsonl rebase-blocker, untracked-vs-tracked collision, May-18-binary launch, and parallel tauri-dev port collision all conspired to produce 'TV-static window'.

Codex adrev round (transcript at dev/adversarial/2026-06-01-convergence-discipline-codex.md on bd-tuxlink-jy6p/convergence-adrev, 3300 lines, agent vetch-plover-ridge) surfaced 21 findings including 2 P0 ship-blockers in my initial design. The revised design adopts Codex's branch-state-machine framing.

Deliverables:

1. ADR: branch lifecycle state machine (active / PR-open / merged-dead / follow-up / disposed) + transition rules. The state machine subsumes orphan-branch detection, post-merge-commit prevention, and bd-state ordering as natural consequences.

2. Git hooks (standard git, NOT Claude-specific so they work for Codex CLI / human shell / non-tool merges): pre-commit + pre-push reject commits/pushes on merged-dead branches.

3. scripts/converge-build.sh — operator-facing single command:
   - Branch classification (open-PR, in-progress-bd, operator-local, bot, merged-dead, unowned-orphan) — refuse only on unowned-orphan (not the broad 'ahead-of-main' predicate)
   - Build from a disposable managed worktree checked out at exactly origin/main (NOT from task-amd-main-ui + restored stash) — eliminates SoT ambiguity
   - Dev-server lease at ~/.config/tuxlink/dev-server.json: inspect owning PID/cwd/branch/SHA on port 1420; require --force-kill-owned to terminate
   - Untracked-vs-tracked decision flow: identical content auto-removes pre-rebase; differing content stops and presents commit/rename/archive/discard choices
   - Audit log: append per-run record (timestamp, origin/main SHA, dirty/overlay status, killed PIDs, install mode, launched PID) for post-hoc forensics
   - The script must enforce its own ADR-0008 lease/session checks because PreToolUse hooks see only the wrapper invocation

4. package.json wrapper: pnpm dev:converged is the documented path; raw pnpm tauri dev gets a deprecation banner. (Codex P1 #7: opt-in safety bypasses muscle memory.)

5. CI scheduled audit: nightly GH Action flagging orphan/dead-branch commits (catches non-Claude-tool bypasses; Codex P1 #9).

6. Test bundle: shell/Python tests against fake fixtures for all 7 known failure modes from the 2026-06-01 session (post-merge commits, stale operator branch, missing pnpm symlink, staged .beads/issues.jsonl, untracked collisions, stale cargo binary, port ownership).

7. CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md updates per the existing documentation propagation contract (ADR is the canonical source; CLAUDE.md is a pointer + recipe summary).

Sub-issues to file as bd-dep children once the ADR lands: hooks, script, package.json wrapper, CI audit, test bundle.

Out of scope for v1: P2 polish (pnpm topology verifier instead of blind rm-rf, named-stash markers, --dry-run mode, UI status-bar SHA). File as P3 follow-ups.

title: fix(mailbox): list_messages returns OS-order (random) — sort by date desc, id asc for deterministic newest-first display
description:
Operator-flagged 2026-06-01: 'We really need mailbox sort. We have search, but messages are sort of randomly ordered right now. That's a load-bearing feature even in an alpha build.'

ROOT CAUSE
src-tauri/src/native_mailbox.rs::Mailbox::list (line 83-107) iterates fs::read_dir on the folder directory and pushes MessageMeta entries in whatever order the filesystem yields. read_dir order is OS/filesystem-dependent (ext4 returns hash-table order; tmpfs returns insertion order; etc.) and is effectively random from the operator's perspective.

FIX
Sort metas before returning:
  Primary key: date descending (newest first — Gmail/inbox convention)
  Tiebreaker: id ascending (deterministic for messages sharing a second-resolution timestamp)

date is RFC 3339 (per parse_rfc3339_secs usage in winlink_backend.rs:634). Parse to seconds, sort by that. Messages with unparseable date sort to the bottom (preserved but not at the top).

SCOPE
Backend sort only (one place fixes it for all folders: Inbox, Outbox, Sent, Archive). Frontend remains the consumer of order; future user-selectable sort can layer on top.

GATES
cargo --lib test for Mailbox::list ordering (new test).
vitest update if MessageList.test or useMailbox.test pins ordering.

Closes the operator's load-bearing-alpha gap on default mailbox ordering.

title: HTML Forms full-parity design + roadmap (4-phase)
description:
Design doc + roadmap for HTML Forms v2 (full WLE feature parity).
Supersedes the v0.1 design at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-30-html-forms-design.md.

Brainstormed 2026-05-31 with dahlia-heron-spruce after operator
critique that the v0.1 forms (PR #177) are "imitations of form rather
than imitations of function" — wire-format work was correct but
operational design treated all 5 forms as fill-in UIs, missing that
several (GPS Position, ICS-309, etc.) should auto-pull / auto-generate
from existing tuxlink data (gpsd, messages_meta).

Key decisions captured in design:
- Scope: Full WLE forms surface parity (hundreds of forms via webview).
- Architecture: Hybrid — 5 native React forms (ICS-213, Bulletin, GPS
  Position, ICS-309, Winlink Check-In) for daily-driver auto-fill UX;
  embedded child webview for the long tail + custom forms.
- Template source: Bundle WLE Standard Forms snapshot; in-app
  auto-update from winlink.org.
- Submit capture: Lazy 127.0.0.1:0 HTTP server (only running while a
  form is open); WLE {FormServer}/{FormPort}/{FormFolder} substitution
  for ecosystem-contract compat; per-open random token; no IPC exposed

title: ARDOP HF dock dead-end on cold start + View menu Show Radio Dock unwired
description:
Two-part bug surfaced during the post-PR-#164 no-radio smoke.

SYMPTOM (operator): Navigate sidebar → Winlink (CMS) → ARDOP HF. Reading pane shows 'ARDOP HF is configured. Use the modem dock on the right to dial a target station.' But the dock is not rendered, and clicking View → Show Radio Dock (or Ctrl+Shift+M) does nothing.

ROOT CAUSE 1 — dock visibility predicate excludes the cold-start ARDOP-HF-view case
src/shell/AppShell.tsx:102
  const dockVisible = modemStatus.state !== 'stopped';

On a cold launch the modem hasn't been spawned, state IS 'stopped', the dock never mounts. The ArdopDock's own render code at lines 191-217 has an explicit 'stopped'-state branch with the Target callsign input + Connect button — designed to render in stopped state — but AppShell's predicate prevents it. Dial form is unreachable; the only way to spawn the modem lives in the hidden dock.

ArdopHfStub.tsx comment cites a 'Phase 4 design' where the dock should also mount when the operator selects the ARDOP HF view. That design was never implemented.

ROOT CAUSE 2 — View menu item unwired
src/shell/chrome/menuModel.ts:54 declares the menu item:
  { id: 'menu:view:radio_dock', label: 'Show Radio Dock', accel: 'Ctrl+Shift+M' }
src/shell/chrome/menuModel.ts:113 declares the accelerator binding.
But src/shell/chrome/dispatchMenuAction.ts has NO case for it — falls through to the 'Stub / not-yet-wired actions: no-op' catch-all. MenuHandlers has no toggleRadioDock field.

FIX
1. AppShell.tsx: dockVisible = (modemStatus.state !== 'stopped') || (selectedConnection?.protocol === 'ardop-hf') || showRadioDockOverride
2. Add useState for showRadioDockOverride + toggleRadioDock handler.
3. Wire menu:view:radio_dock in dispatchMenuAction.ts.
4. Add toggleRadioDock to MenuHandlers interface.
5. Rename label menuModel.ts:54 to 'Toggle Radio Dock' for consistency with the other view-toggle items.
6. Tests: extend AppShell.modemDock.test.tsx + add dispatchMenuAction.test.ts case.

ACCEPTANCE
- Cold-start, navigate to ARDOP HF: dock appears with the dial form.
- Cold-start on Inbox: dock hidden (no clutter).
- Ctrl+Shift+M from any view: dock toggles visible/back-to-auto.
- All existing modemDock tests still pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] ARDOP: Disconnect button likely can't abort an in-flight ARQ connect handshake
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) identified abort-during-connect as a P1 SAFETY gap. The 2026-05-22 memory radio1-bounded-airtime-abort records a prior incident: ~110s runaway connect with no working abort forced the operator to physically power off the radio.

Current behavior: modem_ardop_disconnect → arq_disconnect sends DISCONNECT (the graceful end-of-session command). During an in-flight ARQ connect handshake (status.state = Connecting, thread blocked on TCP recv inside arq_connect()), DISCONNECT likely doesn't return until the 120s CONNECT_DEADLINE elapses — operator can't stop a stuck connect.

ardopcf has an explicit ABORT command (alias DD) — 'immediate interrupt of any in-flight TX' (Host_Interface_Commands.md).

Verification + fix:
1. VERIFY first: spawn ardopcf, start connect to non-existent target via modem_ardop_connect, then call modem_ardop_disconnect during the 120s wait. Does it return promptly or hang?
2. IF hangs: modify modem_ardop_disconnect_inner. Check session.status_snapshot().state — if Spawning/Connecting, send ABORT first (via a new cmd-socket send_line), then DISCONNECT for protocol cleanup.
3. Add backend test: stub transport that simulates a connect-in-progress; verify ABORT path returns promptly.

Honestly verify before any on-air smoke.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-o3f2-ardop-abort-connect. Branch: bd-tuxlink-o3f2/ardop-abort-connect. Created 2026-05-31 02:43:05 UTC by kite-larch-kingfisher.

title: Strip Pat + add native B2F outbound with attachment support
description:
Operator directive 2026-05-30 (mid HTML Forms execution, PR #151): finish the Pat cutover and build the native outbound-with-attachment path that HTML Forms (and any future outbound work) will ride on.

**Why now:** HTML Forms v0.1 spec deliberately chose Path A (Pat REST) for v0.1 outbound because Path B (native B2F + attachments) didn't exist. Operator decided the half-cutover state was costing more than the cutover completion would. See memory: project_pat_complete_strip_directive_2026_05_30.

**Scope:**

1. **Audit Pat surface area** — enumerate every Pat reference: src-tauri/src/{pat_client.rs, pat_config.rs, pat_process.rs}, PatBackend impl in winlink_backend.rs, PatBackendSpawnOptions, bootstrap.rs:386 PatBackend::spawn call, app_backend.rs PatBackend::from_url calls, src-tauri/tests/pat_client_test.rs, Pat sidecar bundling in build/CI scripts, Settings UI Pat-related fields, src-tauri/binaries/ sidecars (pat-*), tauri.conf.json sidecar references.

2. **Build native outbound-with-attachments** in src-tauri/src/winlink/ — this needs a design phase covering:
   - B2F File: header encoding (multipart-equivalent at the B2F protocol layer)
   - lzhuf compression for attachment bodies (the Winlink-Express compression format)
   - Multi-recipient handling (to/cc semantics in native send)
   - Error mapping (BackendError::TransportFailed and friends)
   - Multipart vs single-message attachment-count limits
   - Native send path through the existing winlink::compose::Composer + winlink::transfer::Transfer infrastructure

3. **Replace PatBackend at install sites** — bootstrap.rs + app_backend.rs install NativeBackend exclusively; PatBackend is deleted.

4. **Delete Pat code** — once NativeBackend covers all PatBackend functionality (send_message with + without attachments, list_messages, connect/disconnect, etc.), delete the Pat module tree + tests + sidecar bundle + config UI.

5. **Verify on-air** — operator-driven smoke (CMS Telnet + RF) to confirm native send still works post-cutover. RADIO-1 applies for RF.

**Discipline:**
- Full superpowers:build-robust-features cycle: brainstorm → spec → 5-round adrev (cross-provider: Claude rounds + Codex round per feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev) → plan → 4-round plan review → execute.
- This is hard-to-undo architectural work (Pat removal is permanent); BRF discipline applies in full.

**Blocks:** tuxlink-v1p (HTML Forms v0.1) — PR #151 is paused at T0.2 until this lands. T0.1+T0.2 of the HTML Forms plan are path-agnostic and already committed; everything from T0.3 forward waits on this prereq.

**Worktree path:** to be determined at execution; new bd-<id>/strip-pat-add-native-attachments branch off main.

**References:**
- HTML Forms spec PR #151: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-30-html-forms-design.md §5.1 (Path A vs Path B description)
- HTML Forms plan rev-3: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-30-html-forms-v0.1-plan.md (the path-agnostic tasks survive; pat_client tasks are invalidated)
- Mid-cutover memory: project_pat_fully_replaced_native_client (May 21, partial-cutover snapshot)
- Today's directive memory: project_pat_complete_strip_directive_2026_05_30
- Handoff: dev/handoffs/2026-05-30-heron-tanager-bog-html-forms-paused-pat-strip-prereq.md (to be written)

title: HTML Forms v0.1 — design + implement (read + author + ICS-213 baseline)
description:
Per PR #150 §13.1, HTML Forms is the v0.1 must-have with highest EmComm leverage. Operator (Cameron) explicitly asked to build it autonomously while driving today.

Capability target: §4.1 (render inbound forms) + §4.2 (author ICS-213) from inventory rev-2. Out of scope for v0.1: §4.3 (catalog auto-update) — bundle a curated set of forms in the binary instead.

Investigation findings (from decompiled WLE):
  - WLE form messages: body = human-readable text-rendered template (per .txt 'Msg:' block with <var X> substitutions); attachment = RMS_Express_Form_<name>.xml with structured field values.
  - Tuxlink's existing parse_raw_rfc5322 detects is_form via body.starts_with('<?xml') — that's WRONG for WLE compatibility (XML is in attachment, not body). Need to update detection to look at attachment names matching RMS_Express_Form_*.xml.
  - WLE bundles ~1000+ forms across 18+ categories (ICS USA, ARC, Weather, HICS, state-specific). 3-file pattern per form: <Name>_Initial.html (compose) + <Name>.txt (metadata + Msg template) + <Name>_Viewer.html (display).
  - Forms use embedded WebBrowser + local HTTP server pattern (Form serializes by POST to {FormServer}:{FormPort}). NOT a pattern Tuxlink should copy — native React form widgets are cleaner.

Surfaced 2026-05-30 (yew-cypress-oak). bd-issue 95z (inventory) is parent context.
notes: PR #177 ready for review. Phases 0-11 shipped (Phase 9 reversed from deferred after operator pointed me at .claude/worktree-archives/RMS-personal-install-*.zip). 2 Codex cross-provider rounds, 9 findings, 8 applied + 1 forward-rolled then applied. Workspace clippy clean (resolved tuxlink-o7d4 incidentally). Operator next: live smokes T11.2-T11.5 + browser smoke of Compose form flow. v0.1.1 follow-ups: per-form reply mappings for Phase 9 forms; catalog browser.

title: Pi-side Bluetooth ACL Page-Timeout: Pi can't connect to UV-Pro (EHOSTDOWN) — blocks all on-Pi radio work
description: Pandora's BlueZ can no longer open an ACL to the UV-Pro (38:D2:00:01:55:5C). Every connect path fails: l2ping/raw-RFCOMM-socket/bluetoothctl = 'Host is down'/EHOSTDOWN; GUI = 'no usable services' (expected for bare SPP); sdptool returns nothing. DECISIVE: btmon shows 'Connect Complete -> Status: Page Timeout (0x04)' — the radio doesn't answer the Pi's BR/EDR page (below SPP/RFCOMM/bond). Pair-OK/connect-fail split (re-pair SUCCEEDS, connect page-times-out) points at Pi-side page params/timing. wlan0 down so coexistence ruled out. Tried+failed: restart bluetoothd, hciconfig reset, rfkill cycle, full hci_uart_bcm unbind/rebind+firmware-reload, Codex#1 btmgmt le-off cycle (btmgmt hangs interactively - le-off latch unconfirmed). Radio is known-good (Winlink Express + WoAD use it as raw-KISS BT TNC). Codex consulted (dev/adversarial/2026-05-23-bt-rfcomm-unreachable-codex.md). RESUME: handoff 2026-05-23-gully-marsh-maple-rf-harness-bt-pagetimeout.md §2 — Codex#2 (clear /var/lib/bluetooth device+cache dirs + re-pair), fresh inquiry before connect, else reboot. Blocks tuxlink-4ef on-air test + rfcomm-rx-dump transport test.

title: Close the abort-before-write TOCTOU: disarm the serial/Bluetooth transport on abort (not just flag-gate)
description: Codex adrev of tuxlink-2y4 (commit ea8ac94, 2026-05-22, agent marsh-hemlock-lichen) found a check-then-write race in AbortableByteLink::write (link.rs:80): the abort flag is loaded, THEN inner.write runs — if abort is set in that window, one in-flight KISS frame (a ~20-byte SABM) still keys the radio. BOUNDED, not a runaway: datalink::connect is hard-capped at <=2 SABMs + connect_timeout, so the worst case is ONE extra short SABM if Cancel lands in a sub-microsecond window; the 110s runaway (the original RADIO-1 incident) is fixed. Two related findings: (a) TCP-KISS (link.rs:139) is not wrapped in AbortableByteLink at all — its abort is via socket shutdown, same TOCTOU class (lower concern: TCP is loopback/Dire Wolf). (b) connect_timeout is honored only within ByteLink::read's poll granularity (~200ms via LINK_POLL_TIMEOUT) — fine for all current transports (TCP/serial/RfcommSocket all set read+write timeouts), theoretical for a no-timeout link. FIX: wire abort to DISARM the transport (shutdown the RfcommSocket fd / drop the serialport handle) so an in-flight or subsequent write FAILS at the OS layer, eliminating the race — mirror TCP's abort_handle shutdown for the socket/serial paths. TDD hardware-free; on-air verification operator-run (RADIO-1). Raw adrev: dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-radio1-safety-bundle-codex.md (gitignored, local).
notes:
Reopened 2026-06-02 (taiga-arroyo-clover): PR #225 shipped + merged but operator decision is to revert — the fix changes RF-path abort behavior and cannot be agent-verified under RADIO-1. Revert PR incoming. Re-land after an on-air verification cycle against real Bluetooth + serial hardware.

title: Selector host/transport changes don't reach the live backend without an app restart
description: Found in #124 (tuxlink-3pb) browser smoke 2026-05-22. Operator selected cms-z (config persisted host=cms-z.winlink.org + CmsSsl) but the connect went to PRODUCTION (TLS handshake + 'use cms-z' prod rejection). cms-z has NO TLS (8773 filtered) so a cms-z dial cannot handshake => the connect used a different host than configured. PRIME HYPOTHESIS (unconfirmed): NativeBackend caches config:Config at construction (winlink_backend.rs:433, set in new() ~481); native_connect/resolve_cms_host (~1205) use the cached self.config, and config_set_connect persists to the FILE but does NOT refresh the live backend, so host/transport changes only take effect after an app RESTART. Fits the pid-change timeline (399284 cached cms-z -> cms-z:8773 timeouts; restart -> 423368 cached server.winlink.org -> server:8773 handshake+reject; operator set host=cms-z (file) but 423368 kept cached server -> attempt 2 hit prod). VERIFY FIRST: does the connect read_config() fresh or use self.config? FIX: config_set_connect (or connect path) must refresh the live backend so UI selections apply without restart. Blocks the selector feature; spans #122 config_set_connect + backend arch. Don't merge #124 until fixed.

title: RFCOMM socket transport: TX works but RX broken — relay reply never received (needs bounded on-air byte capture)
description: The new RFCOMM-socket transport (tuxlink-nx2) keys the radio (TX works) but connect() NEVER receives the relay's UA/DM, so it loops the full n2_retries. The OLD serialport-TTY path (connect_serial) was PROVEN to receive (the W7MOT-6 DM in the chasm-sorrel-glade on-air session). So the socket introduced a no-RX regression. Codex + main-loop agree this can't be diagnosed from source — needs raw byte evidence: add timestamped hex logging at RfcommSocket::read/write, then ONE bounded+abortable on-air dial. If the socket gets 0 bytes while the relay replies -> RFCOMM/SPP control-line/service issue (or fall back to the proven TTY); if bytes arrive but don't decode -> KISS/AX.25 decode bug. NOTE: the prior 'serialport termios teardown' diagnosis that motivated the socket pivot was never proven; the Broken-pipe episodes are better explained by stale rfcomm bind / wrong channel / wrong-worktree build. Depends on the RADIO-1 safety bundle landing first (no on-air until bounded+abortable).
notes: Instrumented on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate: timestamped hex RX/TX trace at RfcommSocket::read/write, opt-in via TUXLINK_RFCOMM_TRACE. Awaiting the operator's ONE bounded+abortable on-air dial (RADIO-1) — now safe to run after tuxlink-2y4 landed. Diagnostic: TX-but-no-RX => transport/SPP; RX-but-no-decode => KISS/AX.25.

title: Native client is opaque: emit the raw B2F protocol exchange to the session log (Raw output toggle)
description: Observability regression from the Pat->native cutover. Pat (wl2k-go) printed the full wire dialogue ([WL2K-...], ;PQ, ;FW, SID, ;PR, FF/FQ, session stats) to stderr, which the session log ingested (append_pat_line). The native client (winlink_backend native_connect -> telnet::connect_and_exchange) performs the real B2F exchange (handshake.rs) but only emits ~3 genericized progress() summary lines; it never tees the raw wire lines into the session log. Result: 'Raw output' shows nothing raw for native sessions -> operator cannot verify a real session happened (looks generic + too fast). Fix: tee each raw protocol line (both directions) from the native wire I/O into the durable session log + broadcast, so the Raw output toggle shows the genuine Express-style trace; keep Human as the projection. Gate with an end-to-end client test against cms-z telnet asserting the log contains the real banner + tuxlink SID + FF/FQ (NOT a mocked stream). Found 2026-05-22 by spruce-marten-cardinal; operator-directed.

title: Packet: build the REAL end-to-end integration chain (no mocks)
description: The packet backend (P1 codec, P2 datalink state machine, P3 Winlink integration) and the P4 UI exist + unit/loopback tests pass, but NOTHING has been run end-to-end — not against a real KISS modem, not on hardware, not on-air. The operator running the app found the UI shipped as a non-functional mockup (false 'Listening' status, 127.0.0.1 leaking into USB/BT, duplicated digipeater text, no device selector, Listen entirely unwired). This session fixed those (PR #115) but the feature is still not demonstrably working. BUILD the real integration chain so a connect/listen actually completes against a real KISS modem (TCP/Dire Wolf is the most-complete transport — make it genuinely work first), with real device/transport flows. Operator directive 2026-05-22: NO mock harnesses (they get wired to prod wrong or aren't useful) — build the feature; validate by RUNNING it, not by declaring done.
notes: PROVEN end-to-end (swallow-kite-isthmus, 2026-05-22, PR #115): two real NativeBackend instances complete a full AX.25 connect (SABM/UA) + B2F message transfer over a real TCP+KISS wire — message crosses dialer-outbox -> answerer-inbox. Test: packet_two_real_peers_complete_a_connect_and_b2f_over_tcp_kiss (winlink_backend.rs). NO mocks: every layer is shipping code; only a transparent byte-relay stands in for the TNC+RF PHY. Fixed the headline bug it surfaced: the Answer(master) B2F handshake sent no '>' prompt + read with the wrong terminator -> deadlock (grounded vs wl2k-go; build_master_handshake + read_slave_handshake). Codex round then found + fixed 3 RF-real defects the relay masked: BLOCKER BufReader-vs-Ok(0) premature-EOF (BlockingB2fStream adapter), answer() not pushing KISS params, CRLF peek. 304 lib tests green. REMAINING before release-done: (1) operator app-smoke (tauri dev) to confirm UI->command wiring; (2) independent-decoder validation vs Dire Wolf/kernel-AX.25 (new follow-up issue) since peer-to-peer shares the codec; (3) hardware USB/BT (tuxlink-jvp); (4) on-air (RADIO-1, operator). Left in_progress for operator to confirm app-smoke.

title: Wizard completion is a dead-end: shows a dev placeholder, never mounts the shell (no restart-free hand-off)
description: On reaching state.step==='complete', Wizard.tsx renders the literal dev string 'Wizard complete — main shell mounts via App.tsx routing.' App.tsx reads wizardCompleted ONCE at startup via get_wizard_completed and mounts <Wizard/> with NO completion callback + no reload/re-probe, so finishing the wizard never mounts <AppShell/> — the user is stranded on the placeholder until an app RESTART (which re-reads wizard_completed=true). Fix: App passes onComplete=()=>setWizardCompleted(true) to <Wizard>; WizardInner fires it via useEffect when step==='complete'; delete the dev placeholder. Found in post-merge smoke 2026-05-22. Bundle with the wizard-styling fix into one 'first-run wizard: make it shippable' PR.

title: AX.25 1200-baud packet transport (v0.1 headline feature)
description: Add AX.25 1200-baud packet as a Winlink transport in the native client: dial an RMS Packet gateway (secure-login), dial a peer (P2P, no auth), and LISTEN/ANSWER an inbound peer call. KISS byte-pipes over TCP (Dire Wolf/SoundModem), USB serial, and Bluetooth serial (treated as a serial device). tuxlink implements host-side AX.25 v2.x connected-mode (SABM/UA/I-frames/T1, mod-8, FCS, 0-2 digipeaters) over KISS — the RMS Express model (wl2k-go delegates to kernel/AGWPE; we do not). Global sticky station SSID (operate as e.g. N7CPZ-7); B2F identity stays the base callsign. Frequency is note-only (rig control deferred to tuxlink-5jb). Design spec: docs/design/2026-05-22-ax25-packet-v0.1-design.md. Protocol ground truth: docs/design/ax25-packet-protocol-findings.md. Rides on integrating native-client (bd-tuxlink-0ic) + the v0.0.1 UI onto the trunk + AppImage (tuxlink-cs7). The AX.25 connected-mode engine is correctness-critical -> full robustness pipeline (TDD + cross-provider Codex adrev). writing-plans will decompose into child tasks.
notes: BASE: origin/main (d42af8d, PR #110) ALREADY contains the merged native client + v0.0.1 UI — NO Phase-0 integration prerequisite. Build directly on origin/main in worktrees/bd-tuxlink-7fr-ax25-packet. native-tls already a dep; add serialport for USB/BT. COORDINATION: tuxlink-686 (position subsystem, in_progress) also touches config.rs, winlink_backend.rs, ui_commands.rs, lib.rs, ribbon — the AX.25 link-layer plan (winlink/ax25/, new module) has ZERO overlap and goes first; the integration/UI plans rebase onto main after 686 lands. Decomposition: P1 wire codec / P2 datalink+transports / P3 winlink-packet integration / P4 UI.

title: Abort/Cancel control for an in-flight CMS connection
description: Operator: no way to cancel a connect that's slow/stalled. Need an Abort control in the dashboard (next to Connect, shown while connecting) + a backend cancel path. The connect runs in tokio::task::spawn_blocking (winlink_backend.rs); cancelling blocking socket I/O needs a shutdown handle — hold the TcpStream (or a shutdown signal) so an abort command can .shutdown(Both) to unblock the blocked connect/read, returning status to Disconnected. New cms_abort command + a frontend button bound to the Connecting state.
close_reason: Abort control shipped: WinlinkBackend::abort() shuts down a try_clone'd connect socket to unblock slow TLS/login/exchange phases; aborting flag maps the result to Cancelled (Disconnected, 'CMS connection aborted.'); race guard shuts down on-register if abort landed during the un-abortable TCP-connect window. cms_abort command + ribbon Abort button (shown while connecting). 160 lib + 354 frontend tests green, tsc clean. Commit 1163f04. Full mid-connect cancel = operator smoke (recipe in handoff).

title: Native CMS connect can stall silently (no connect timeout, no progress logging)
description: Operator hit a stall against cms-z: session log shows 'Connecting to the CMS (CmsSsl)…' then nothing. winlink/telnet.rs connect_stream uses TcpStream::connect with NO connect timeout (blocks ~75-130s on a dead endpoint); the 60s read/write timeout only applies post-connect; TLS handshake + telnet_login + run_exchange can each hang up to 60s; and there is no per-step progress logging between 'Connecting…' and the result, so any slow phase reads as a dead stall. Fix: TcpStream::connect_timeout (fail-fast, ~10-15s), per-step progress log lines (TCP connected / TLS established / secure login / proposals), and consider phase-appropriate timeouts. Relates to the abort-control + the earlier-confirmed working TLS path (so pinpoint via native_cms_probe).
notes: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED (granite-finch-spruce, 2026-05-21): raw TCP differential — cms-z:8773(TLS)=FILTERED(15s timeout, =operator ETIMEDOUT); cms-z:8772(plaintext)=OPEN 0.07s; production server.winlink.org has BOTH 8772+8773. native_cms_probe over cms-z:8772 plaintext completes a FULL session (TCP+telnet login+B2F+MD5 secure-login+proposals). So cms-z does NOT expose TLS on 8773; commit 7c50359 set dev host=cms-z but left default transport=CmsSsl/8773. Fix scope: (1) TcpStream::connect_timeout(~15s) in winlink/telnet.rs connect_stream; (2) per-step progress logging via progress sink injected into NativeBackend at construction (NOT log_tx broadcast — that drain is dead per bootstrap.rs:392); (3) dev transport coherence — mirror probe's TUXLINK_CMS_PLAINTEXT/TUXLINK_CMS_PORT env overrides in native_connect, keep CmsSsl as production/persisted default.
close_reason: Fixed: connect_timeout(15s) fail-fast + per-step progress logging + dev transport coherence (TUXLINK_CMS_PLAINTEXT/PORT). Live-validated via native_cms_probe: cms-z:8773/TLS fails in 16s (was 75-130s), 8772/plaintext completes + prints progress. 156 lib tests green. Commit 0de773a.

title: Native Rust Winlink client (telnet+CMS+B2F+mailbox) — replace Pat; milestone 1: telnet
description: Operator directive 2026-05-21: replace Pat with a clean-room NATIVE Rust Winlink client; stop investing in the discarded Go sidecar. Escalates ADR 0011 (fork-and-patch) to full native replacement (needs a new ADR to record the escalation). Brainstorm/decision ALREADY done: Codex adversarial review 2026-05-18 (dev/adversarial/2026-05-18-pat-greenfield-vs-keep-codex.md, gitignored) recommended native Rust (Option B), STAGED, with Pat as oracle/fallback during migration; ~4-6k LOC for telnet+mailbox+API. Build behind the EXISTING WinlinkBackend trait (src-tauri/src/winlink_backend.rs, 8 async methods). Milestone 1 scope = telnet-CMS transport + secure-login + B2F session state machine + FBB/lzhuf codec + native mailbox. AX.25/KISS and native VARA modem are SEPARATE later milestones. Clean-room here = clean Rust impl of the FIXED B2F interop protocol (MUST speak real Winlink); wl2k-go + Pat fork are READ-ONLY reference + test oracle, ship zero Go.
design: Pipeline per ADR 0011 §3 (build-robust-features): committed design spec (promote gitignored Codex design + fill the 4 Codex-flagged gaps: explicit test-oracle plan; native mailbox migration from Pat .b2f; AX.25 hardware matrix [deferred]; native-VARA clean-room boundary [deferred]) -> 5-round cross-provider adrev (>=1 Codex) -> writing-plans-enhanced -> TDD -> Codex on diff -> PR. KEY: protocol core is in github.com/la5nta/wl2k-go (FBB 2.6k, lzhuf 937, telnet 180, mailbox 287 LOC) + n8jja/Pat-Vara, NOT Pat internal/. Pat retained as oracle/fallback until native reaches telnet parity + soak; do NOT delete prematurely (would break the just-merged M2 mailbox display).
notes: 2026-05-21 basil-vale-thistle: native Winlink client BUILT, LIVE-VALIDATED, and APP CUT OVER (Pat no longer spawned). Full protocol library (winlink/: message, proposal, lzhuf [byte-exact], transfer, secure, wire, handshake, session [send/receive turns + run_exchange + turn cap], telnet [plaintext + TLS], compose) + native_mailbox (Pat-independent store) + functional NativeBackend (send→outbox, list/read from store, connect runs the real exchange) + cms_connect command + ribbon Connect button. Bootstrap installs NativeBackend (not Pat); Pat code retained #[allow(dead_code)]. LIVE-VALIDATED vs real CMS: plaintext full session on cms-z; TLS full transport on server.winlink.org:8773. ~32 commits. 142 lib + 12 integration + 311 frontend tests pass. Deps: md-5, native-tls. ONE production blocker: register client name 'tuxlink' with Winlink (request drafted, inline + dev/winlink-client-registration-request.md; production rejects unregistered SIDs). Fixed this session: real identity by default (fixture opt-in), Mac glyph→Ctrl, connect-result→session log. Full handoff: dev/handoffs/2026-05-21-basil-vale-thistle-native-client-shipped.md

title: PatProcess::spawn hardening: enforce announce timeout (no hang) + drain/null Pat stdout (no deadlock)
description: Surfaced by Codex adrev R1 on tuxlink-22l (2026-05-20). Two pre-existing robustness bugs in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs::spawn: (#7) the 10s announce deadline is NOT actually enforced — it uses blocking BufReader::read_line, so if Pat stays alive but emits no newline the read blocks forever despite the Instant deadline check (the deadline is only re-checked between lines). Fix: read stderr via a timeout thread / recv_timeout / non-blocking poll so the deadline is real. (#18) Pat stdout is piped (Stdio::piped()) but never read, so a chatty Pat can fill the OS pipe buffer and block the child. Fix: set stdout to Stdio::null() (or drain it). tuxlink-22l's bootstrap depends on a spawn that cannot hang.
notes: Codex pqg adrev R1 #2 (2026-05-20) ALSO flagged a Child leak in PatProcess::spawn: on announce timeout the child is killed WITHOUT wait() (zombie), and a pid_file write failure after spawn drops Child without killing it. Fix alongside the announce-timeout/stdout-drain hardening: wrap Child in an armed guard right after cmd.spawn(), kill+wait on every error path, disarm only when returning PatProcess. VERIFY #88's spawn rewrite covers this before closing xyd.
close_reason: Delivered by PR #88 (merged 2026-05-21T12:59Z). 22l: PatBackend::spawn + app-start bootstrap. xx3: SessionLogState ring buffer (Task A). xyd: spawn hardening verified by peregrine-spruce-knoll — recv_timeout announce deadline (real, not in-loop Instant), Stdio::null stdout drain, Child kill+wait on every error path (L193/207/224) + impl Drop (L284), no bare ? between spawn and success return.

title: Tray restore broken on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: window hidden-to-tray can't be brought back
description:
Found 2026-05-20 (pika-glade-bluff) during operator review of the cbz tauri-dev run. Close-to-tray hides the main window (lib.rs CloseRequested -> window.hide() + prevent_close), but on this Pi (Wayland + wf-panel-pi) the operator cannot bring the window back from the tray — it 'persists but is not accessible'. Pre-existing Task-8 (tuxlink-rit) behavior; tray.rs is untouched by tuxlink-cbz. The handoff only verified tray->Quit, never tray->restore on this compositor.

LIKELY CAUSES (tray.rs):
1. Left-click restore relies on on_tray_icon_event TrayIconEvent::Click{Left,Up} -> show_main_window. wlroots/SNI panels (wf-panel-pi) commonly do NOT forward left-click 'Activate' to the SNI host, so left-click never fires -> nothing happens.
2. The right-click menu 'Show/Hide Window' (tray:show_hide) calls toggle_main_window, which branches on window.is_visible(). After a Wayland window.hide(), is_visible() is unreliable; if it reports stale 'visible', the toggle hides again and the window never re-shows.
3. window.hide()->show() reliability on Wayland/WebKitGTK is itself a known Tauri pain point (window may not re-map).

FIX IDEAS:
- Replace the visibility-TOGGLE menu item with an explicit, unconditional 'Show Window' that calls show() + unminimize() + set_focus() (and a separate 'Hide'), so restore never depends on is_visible(). show_main_window already does the unconditional show — route the menu through it.
- Add unminimize() to the show path.
- Don't depend on left-click activation as the only obvious restore (keep it, but the menu must be reliable).
- Verify on the actual Wayland/wf-panel-pi setup (operator-run; agent can't see the native window) AND on X11 for regression.

IMPACT: a window closed to tray can become unrecoverable except by killing/restarting the process — bad for an emcomm tool whose whole point is surviving close-to-tray mid-session. P1.
acceptance_criteria: From a hidden-to-tray state on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: right-click tray -> Show Window reliably restores + focuses the main window every time (no double-click, no inverted toggle). Restore also works after multiple hide/show cycles. Verified by the operator on the Pi's actual compositor; X11 path not regressed. Pat child process still survives close-to-tray (the feature that motivated hide-on-close).
notes: WORSE THAN RESTORE-TOGGLE (pika-glade-bluff 2026-05-20): on the operator's current Wayland/wf-panel-pi session the tray icon does NOT appear AT ALL (no SNI host -> TrayIconBuilder.build() silently no-ops). So close-to-tray (lib.rs CloseRequested -> hide()) strands the window: process stays alive (confirmed target/debug/tuxlink running hidden) with NO GUI path back. Only recovery is pkill + relaunch. COMPOUNDING: tauri-plugin-window-state (lib.rs:40, default flags) persists VISIBILITY for ALL windows incl. main -> a clean exit while hidden would save visible=false and the NEXT launch could start hidden too (not yet triggered: no .window-state.json saved yet). REQUIRED FIX must guarantee recoverability, e.g.: (a) minimize instead of hide on Linux (stays in compositor window list), OR (b) verify the tray actually registered before enabling hide-on-close (else fall back to minimize/real-close), OR (c) a global show-window shortcut; AND exclude StateFlags::VISIBLE from window-state for the main window so it can never restore hidden. Title broadened: not just 'restore broken' but 'tray may be absent -> close strands window'. Still P1 (arguably higher — strands an emcomm app).

title: v0.0.1 visual-fidelity gap: global CSS layer never implemented (starter App.css, no reset, undefined --tux-* tokens, light form controls)
description: Found at M2 smoke 2026-05-20. The main-UI build gated logic + functional structure but had NO visual-fidelity gate, so the GLOBAL design layer was never implemented and the shipped UI diverges sharply from docs/design/v0.0.1-ux-mockups.md + docs/design/mockups/images/. Specific VERIFIED findings (worktree bd-tuxlink-8zg-integration-wiring @ feat 3a94790): (1) NO global reset — default body{margin:8px} offsets the 100vw/100vh .app-shell from top/left + overflows viewport ('floating' bug); add *,*::before,*::after{box-sizing:border-box} + html,body,#root{margin:0;height:100%}. (2) src/App.css is the unmodified create-tauri-app starter — light :root (#0f0f0f on #f6f6f6), .logo/.container/.row dead rules, light form-control theming -> inputs/buttons render LIGHT on the dark shell. Replace with the real design system. (3) The --tux-* design tokens (--tux-bg/fg/border/hover/accent-bg/badge) are used as fallbacks in component CSS but NEVER defined centrally -> no theme source of truth; define them on :root from the mock palette. (4) No box-sizing:border-box -> paddings inflate widths past grid tracks -> cramming/overlap. (5) index.html <title> still 'Tauri + React + Typescript'. NOTE: per-component CSS (AppShell grid, DashboardRibbon, StatusBar, MessageView, Compose) is largely sound + matches the mock LAYOUT; the gap is the global foundation + mock-fidelity polish (typography/spacing/aesthetic), not a rebuild. Suggest two tiers: (A) global-CSS foundation fix (reset/box-sizing/tokens/base+form theming/retitle) — fixes the reported floating+cramming+theme-clash; (B) a frontend-design fidelity pass against the mocks. PROCESS follow-up: add a visual-smoke / design-fidelity gate to the build pipeline so this can't recur.
notes:
FOUNDATION SLICE LANDED via PR #85 (commit 0c01f75): global reset + box-sizing (fixes floating + cramming), central --tux-* tokens + amber accent from mock-d, dark base + form-control theming (fixes light wizard/forms), retitle. title: Keyring integration tests write to the operator's REAL system keyring (XDG_DATA_HOME/HOME not isolated)
description: ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-05-20 keyring breakage. tuxlink's keyring integration tests call entry.set_password() against the real Secret Service: wizard_integration_test.rs:92/209 ('should write to real keyring') and wizard_persist_cms_test.rs:171. The XdgGuard (wizard_integration_test.rs:47-53) isolates ONLY XDG_CONFIG_HOME. gnome-keyring stores under XDG_DATA_HOME / HOME (~/.local/share/keyrings/), which is NOT isolated; dbus-run-session isolates only the D-Bus session bus, not the on-disk keyring files. So 'isolated' keyring tests AND any setup recipe (e.g. SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/user/1000/keyring/ssh with a typed password) reach straight into the operator's REAL, cross-project-shared (geographica also uses secret-tool against this same login keyring) system keyring. On 2026-05-20 a manual setup one-liner (bash history line 1809) re-keyed the operator's login keyring with an unrecoverable password, breaking secret access for BOTH tuxlink and geographica until a reset. FIX: (1) keyring tests MUST set a throwaway HOME (or XDG_DATA_HOME) so gnome-keyring uses a temp keyring dir, IN ADDITION to dbus-run-session; assert the test never resolves to ~/.local/share/keyrings. (2) Provide a documented safe headless-keyring-test recipe (ephemeral HOME + dbus-run-session + gnome-keyring-daemon --unlock with EMPTY password inside the sandbox). (3) Purge any setup guidance that runs --unlock against the real keyring. Cross-project hazard; bites the shared-CI/dev-environment transfer goal.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-cnd-keyring-test-isolation. Branch: bd-tuxlink-cnd/keyring-test-isolation. Created 2026-05-20 17:55:29 UTC by fen-sycamore-falcon.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #87 (merged to feat/v0.0.1). Added assert_keyring_isolated() fail-closed guard to all 3 #[ignore]d real-keyring tests + a safe non-ignored unit-test regression guard + safe headless recipe in testing-pitfalls.md §7. cargo test green; the 3 real-keyring tests stay #[ignore]d.

title: Define WinlinkBackend trait — v0.5 prep Step 1 (Pat greenfield refactor)
description:
Operator decision 2026-05-18 (Codex + Cameron convergence): tuxlink moves off Pat to native Rust at v0.5 via staged replacement (not flag-day). Step 1 is the architectural boundary — define a WinlinkBackend trait that decouples tuxlink's UI/config layer from the Pat HTTP sidecar so future native + Pat implementations can co-exist behind one interface.

Trait surface (from current Pat usage in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs + pat-client integration):
- list_messages(folder: Folder) -> Vec<MessageMeta>
- read_message(id: MessageId) -> Message
- send_message(msg: OutboundMessage) -> Result<MessageId>
- connect(transport: TransportConfig) -> Result<SessionHandle>
- disconnect(session: SessionHandle) -> Result<()>
- status() -> BackendStatus
- stream_log() -> impl Stream<Item = LogLine>

Two conceptual impls:
1. PatBackend — wraps current HTTP-to-Pat-sidecar. Lands in this issue's PR.
2. NativeBackend — placeholder/stub in this issue; fleshed out in Steps 3-10.

Discipline: this IS a hard-to-undo architectural decision (the trait shape constrains every future backend implementation), so build-robust-features applies — BUT TIGHTLY SCOPED per the operator's 2026-05-18 framing:
- Brainstorm: 1 hour max (concept only — error model, async vs sync, session-handle ownership, log-stream cancellation)
- Spec: trait definition + behavior contract + 5-10 test cases (NOT 24+)
- >=1 cross-provider adrev round (NOT 5 — Codex review on the trait surface only)
- Plan: trivial (trait file + stub NativeBackend + PatBackend wrapper around current pat-client code)
- Ship as one PR

Follow-up steps (each gets its own bd issue when this one merges):
2. Freeze Pat backend as reference (PatBackend implements the trait)
3. Build native mailbox + B2F parser/writer with golden fixtures from Pat / wl2k-go
4. Build B2F session state machine over generic async IO
5. Build CMS telnet client + fake CMS harness
6. Pat-vs-native parity tests over same fixtures
7. Operator-only live CMS smoke gate
8. AX.25/KISS layer (KISS = protocol compliance per memory feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability)
9. Native VARA as separate milestone (clean-room: reverse-engineering writeups read as inverse spec, NOT adoption guide)
10. Default native after parity+soak; remove Pat sidecar after one fallback release

Memories to read before starting:
- feedback_discipline_triage_rule (this IS architectural; pipeline applies, but tightly scoped)
- feedback_clean_sheet_concepts_only
- feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability
- project_v05_modem_design_posture
- project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand
- feedback_no_atomic_decisions_to_operator

Replaces nothing in v0.0.1 — this is purely v0.5 prep. v0.0.1 continues to ship the Pat sidecar via the cred-handling fork (PR-A merged + PR-B merged); the trait wraps that existing surface without disturbing the shipped wizard or PatProcess flow.

title: Task 2 config implementation (AMD-1 nested schema + AMD-11 + validate_identity + write_config_atomic) — HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster plan
description:
Surfaced by 2026-05-18 wizard-cluster plan-review R1 friction lens (P0-1, P0-3) and verified against shipped src-tauri/src/config.rs.

Current state of src-tauri/src/config.rs (verified 2026-05-18):
- HAS flat schema with: callsign, grid_square, pat_mbo_address, winlink_password_present, wizard_completed (all top-level)
- MISSING: nested connect/identity/privacy structs per AMD-1
- STILL HAS: winlink_password_present per AMD-11 should-be-dropped
- MISSING: validate_identity() function (per AMD-1 loose-validator semantics)
- MISSING: write_config_atomic() function (per Task 2 spec; used by wizard_persist_cms in wizard-cluster spec §3.2)

Gap pattern: AMD-1 (2026-05-17) was a plan-text amendment; the corresponding CODE update to config.rs never shipped. Same gap exists for AMD-11 (2026-05-18 field drop). The plan amendments document intent; code amendments implement it — they don't auto-cascade.

Scope:
1. Update Config struct to nested shape per AMD-1: connect{connect_to_cms, transport}, identity{callsign, identifier, grid}, privacy{gps_state, position_precision}, pat_mbo_address.
2. Drop winlink_password_present per AMD-11.
3. Implement validate_identity(s: &str) -> bool per AMD-1's loose-validator semantics (non-empty + no internal whitespace + ≤32 chars + ASCII-printable).
4. Implement write_config_atomic(config: &Config) -> Result<(), ConfigError> using tempfile + std::fs::rename to /tuxlink/config.json.
5. Update all existing call sites + tests in config_test.rs.
6. Pitfalls entry: document the 'plan amendment != code amendment' gap pattern (in implementation-pitfalls.md as a new entry; cross-cutting discipline).

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev — no upstream skip.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 wizard impl plan (which assumes this work shipped). Without this landing first, wizard impl plan Phase 0 will catch the gap AND Phase 3.2 wizard_persist_cms will fail to compile (calls undefined validate_identity + write_config_atomic).

Also flagged by R3 P0-2 (§5.13 schema version downgrade refusal — implemented in write_config_atomic's pre-write read-check).
notes: PR #66 opened 2026-05-18 (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/66) on bd-tuxlink-4mt/task-2-config-impl. 7 commits: Phase 0-7. 34/34 tests pass. close on merge via deliverable.

title: Task 3 (PatProcess) amendment — render Pat's non-secret config at spawn time
description:
HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster impl (tuxlink-ln3 / Tasks 9/10/11/11.5). Surfaced by Codex R5 P1 on wizard-cluster spec adrev §7.3 item 4.

Pre-cred-handling: Pat's config.json was pre-existing or operator-managed; PatProcess just spawned the binary.
Post-cred-handling (PR-A merged): Pat's config.json no longer carries the WL2K password (deleted from cfg.SecureLoginPassword); Pat reads password from OS keyring. BUT Pat still expects its own config.json with non-secret fields (callsign, MBO address, transport selection).

Wizard now writes: (1) tuxlink config.json + (2) keyring entry. Wizard does NOT write Pat's config. Without something filling that gap, Pat spawns but has no callsign/MBO config = nonfunctional.

Scope: amend Task 3 (PatProcess in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs) to render Pat's config.json from tuxlink's config + the keyring callsign convention, at Pat-spawn time. Likely involves: read tuxlink config; build Pat config struct/json; write to Pat's expected path (~/.config/pat/config.json); spawn Pat.

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev (no upstream skip). Pipeline: brainstorm (small; design is settled by cred-handling spec + AMD-13 + this gap) → spec → 5-round adrev → spec revision → plan-write → 4-round plan-review → revision → TDD impl.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 impl plan (wizard cluster) — without this amendment landing, the wizard impl would ship but Pat couldn't operate. Wizard impl plan should document this as a HARD prerequisite if not yet done.

title: Fork-setup: create tuxlink-pat repo, mirror upstream, document build + sync workflow
description:
Per ADR 0011 §'Implementation follow-ups (queued as bd tasks)'. Operational setup for the tuxlink-pat fork:

1. Operator-decided: repo home (personal account / new org / vendored subdirectory)
2. Create the repo + initial commit (la5nta/pat upstream at chosen point in time)
3. Set up branch protection (no force-push to main; PR-required for merges)
4. Document the build pipeline: how tuxlink's src-tauri/ build sources Pat from the fork (build-from-source vs prebuilt-binary-release vs git submodule)
5. Document the upstream-sync workflow (cadence, who runs it, conflict-resolution policy)
6. Update tuxlink's src-tauri/ build to source Pat from the fork instead of upstream binary download

Full build-robust-features pipeline per ADR 0011 §3 (brainstorm with Cameron for the operator-decision points, then 5-round cross-provider adrev, then writing-plans-enhanced, then plan-review-cycle, then impl).

Blocks: cred-handling refactor (needs fork to exist), v0.0.1 plan amendments (needs fork layout to document).
