title: Cred-handling refactor: Pat reads WL2K from OS keyring (first tuxlink-pat patch)
description:
Per ADR 0011 §2 'First refactor target.' First agentic patch against tuxlink-pat after fork-setup completes.

Scope:
- Pat reads WL2K password from OS keyring (secret-service on Linux Gnome/KDE, Keychain on macOS, CredentialManager on Windows) instead of from config.json
- config.json retains callsign + non-secret config; secrets move out entirely
- Tuxlink wizard (Task 9) writes the password into the keyring directly via Rust keyring crate; wizard never touches a config.json path containing the password
- Live-CMS smoke binary (Task 6) reads from keyring at run time; no env vars, no temp files, no disk persistence beyond keyring

Upstream PR contribution: pursue after fork-side patch ships. Secret-service / Keychain / CredentialManager are platform-native and Pat upstream may welcome the security improvement.

Full build-robust-features pipeline (brainstorm → 5-round cross-provider adrev → writing-plans-enhanced → plan-review-cycle → TDD impl → Codex on impl diff → PR).

Blocks: Task 6 resume (tuxlink-nk7), Task 9 wizard work (tuxlink-ko0).
notes: Phase 10 shipped 2026-05-18 by maple-magpie-oak. PR-B opened as cameronzucker/tuxlink#59 against feat/v0.0.1; bumps external/tuxlink-pat to PR-A merge SHA 4969aa86. Awaits Cameron's merge → bd close tuxlink-mib at that point. Worktree /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-mib-mib-cred-keyring/ is CLEAN; dispose per ADR 0009 post-merge. Plan LDC banner updated to 🚧 In progress.

Pat now reads WL2K credentials from OS keyring; web UI deleted (~20k LoC); 24 unit tests + 2 integration tests in credstore package + 7-test exchange callback regression. Worktree disposal + plan LDC final ✅ flip rolled into next planning cycle.

title: ADR 0011: Fork Pat for tuxlink (cred-handling + ongoing refactor)
description:
Architectural decision triggered 2026-05-18 during Task 6 brainstorm. Cameron rejected continuing to depend on upstream Pat as-is because Pat persists WL2K passwords to its config.json in plaintext, which is a non-negotiable defect for a production system. Bandaging the symptom at each tuxlink call site (env-var workarounds, temp-file mitigations) is bandaid spiraling.

Decision (Cameron, inline): FORK Pat as a tuxlink-owned variant. Refactor cred-handling to use OS keyring as the first patch. Continue refactoring other Pat limitations as discovered, using the full build-robust-features pipeline per refactor. Open to upstream contributions where the fork's changes are accepted upstream.

This ADR captures: context (the bandaid pattern), decision (fork), alternatives considered (continue-as-is / Rust rewrite / hybrid), consequences (Go codebase to maintain, fork-sync discipline, v0.0.1 scope expansion to include fork-setup + cred refactor + plan amendments for affected tasks 5/6/9), and the transition plan.

Light pipeline: decision is made; ADR is a write-up + Codex round + PR. Full build-robust-features pipeline NOT required for the ADR itself (it's documentation of a made decision). Subsequent tasks (fork-setup, cred-refactor, plan amendments) DO use the full pipeline.

title: Bug: get_tuxlink_sessions.py disagrees with block-main-checkout-race.sh on live-session detection
description:
Per towhee-wren-aspen's 2026-05-18 reviewer response (dev/incidents/2026-05-18-main-checkout-race-hook-loop-reviewer-response.md, reviewer Action 3) — the script and the hook read the same .git/session-leases/*.json files with the same 30-min default cutoff, but in the 2026-05-18 incident the script reported 'No live tuxlink sessions in this repo' while the hook was treating a 4-minutes-ago lease as live. They should agree by construction; they don't. This is a port-quality bug independent of the CLAUDE.md framing fix in PR #39 — even after the framing fix, a script that reports 'no live sessions' while the hook denies will continue to confuse future agents who consult it for legitimate informational purposes (e.g., 'is anyone else working in this repo right now?').

Reviewer's likely causes, in priority order:

1. **Timestamp parse failure (most likely).** The hook writes lastSeenUtc using bash's date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ (6-digit microseconds + trailing Z). Python's datetime.fromisoformat is stricter than people expect about fractional-second precision and trailing-Z handling. The script's parse_iso_utc returns None on parse failure, and on None the lease is silently skipped — causing under-reporting without any error message.

   Reproduce:
       ts=$(jq -r .lastSeenUtc .git/session-leases/<any-session-id>.json)
       python3 -c "
       import sys
       sys.path.insert(0, '.claude/scripts')
       from get_tuxlink_sessions import parse_iso_utc
       print(repr(parse_iso_utc('$ts')))
       "

   If None: that's the bug. Fix: align format on both sides (widen parse_iso_utc OR constrain the hook).

2. **Lease dir path mismatch.** The hook resolves the lease dir via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (= .git/session-leases/). The script resolves via Path(__file__).resolve().parent / '..' / '..' / '.claude' / 'session-leases'. If these resolve to different paths, the script reads an empty/stale dir and reports 'no sessions.'

   Reproduce: print both dirs from a test harness; compare.

3. **File-extension/glob mismatch.** Less likely. The script's sorted(lease_dir.glob('*.json')) should pick up every *.json file. If the hook is writing to a dir without .json extension, the script silently skips.

Priority: P1 (blocks future port-quality regressions of same shape; cheap fix; safety-stack relevance). Should land as its own atomic PR after diagnosis confirms which cause applies. No dependencies on PRs #37/#38/#39/#40.
notes: PR #44 opened 2026-05-18 by oak-fjord-swallow. Tasks 1-7 + 9-10 complete; Task 8 (PARITY-1 pitfalls entry) deferred until PR #43 (superseded PR #40 after rebase) merges and brings HOOK-1+LEASE-1 into feat/v0.0.1. Closes via deliverable on merge. Auto-memory feedback_stale_lease_means_worktree.md updated in parallel (out-of-repo).

title: [GRIB] Saildocs request — parameter form, composer to query@saildocs.com, save-attachment flow
description:
Per operator B1 default (PR #283 merged 'resume as you were'). WLE parity = Saildocs only; no Winlink-CMS GRIB; no in-app viewer.

Spec:
1. Form: src/grib/GribRequestPanel.tsx (inline overlay) with controls for:
   - Region: 4 fields (LAT0, LAT1, LON0, LON1) — whole degrees + N/S/E/W per Saildocs format
   - Grid spacing: dlat, dlon (default 2,2)
   - Valid times: comma-separated or range (e.g. '6,12..96'; default '24,48,72')
   - Parameters: multi-select checkboxes from {PRMSL, WIND, HGT, SEATMP, AIRTMP, WAVES} (default PRESS,WIND)
   - Mode: send (one-shot) vs sub (recurring with days= and time= fields)
   - Model: gfs (only one supported for now; Saildocs has more but defer)
2. Composer: builds:
   - To: query@saildocs.com
   - Subject: brief label (operator-editable; default 'GRIB request')
   - Body: 'send gfs:LAT0,LAT1,LON0,LON1|dlat,dlon|VTs|Params' (or 'sub' variant)
   - Route through existing outgoing rails
3. Receipt: response arrives as Private message with GRIB-1 binary attachment. Existing MessageView attachment list renders it; 'Save As...' to disk. NO in-app GRIB viewer for v0.x (operator opens in zyGrib/OpenCPN externally — matches WLE).
4. Menu wire: 'Message → GRIB File Request' (per WLE menu naming).

Tests:
- Form validates region bounds + grid spacing + times syntax
- Composer golden test: known parameter set → expected Saildocs body string
- 'send' vs 'sub' switch composes correctly

Spec reference: https://saildocs.com/gribinfo (canonical syntax). Recap in docs/design/2026-06-02-cms-request-protocol-grounding.md §'GRIB request via Saildocs'.

title: Add dependabot ignore rule for vitest semver-major bumps until ztuv coordinated upgrade lands
description:
Short-term mitigation for tuxlink-ztuv. Closing PR #274 (vitest 2 → 4) doesn't prevent dependabot from re-proposing it every weekly scan. Adding an ignore rule for vitest semver-major updates stops the recurring noise while the deliberate Vite 5 → 6 + vitest 2 → 4 migration (tuxlink-ztuv) is planned and executed.

4-line addition to the npm ecosystem block in .github/dependabot.yml:

  ignore:
    - dependency-name: 'vitest'
      update-types: ['version-update:semver-major']
    - dependency-name: '@vitest/*'
      update-types: ['version-update:semver-major']

Effect:
- Suppresses 3.x and 4.x vitest proposals
- DOES allow vitest 2.x.y minor/patch updates to continue (e.g. 2.1.10, 2.2.0)
- Trivially reverted when ztuv lands (delete the 4 lines)

title: Coordinated upgrade: Vite 5 → 6 + vitest 2 → 4 + @vitest/coverage-v8 2 → 4 (block dependabot from auto-bumping vitest before this lands)
description:
tuxlink-xcay session triage of dependabot PRs closed PR #274 (vitest 2.1.9 → 4.1.8) because vitest 4 requires Vite 6. Our project is on Vite 5.4.21.

Error reproduced locally:
  Error [ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED]: Package subpath './module-runner' is not defined by 'exports' in vite/package.json
  ... imported from vitest/dist/chunks/cli-api.BfdDOPPI.js

vitest 4 imports vite/module-runner internally; that subpath was added in Vite 6.

This task: coordinate the upgrade as one PR:
1. Bump vite ^5.3.0 → ^6.x in package.json
2. Bump vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8 → ^4.x (the dependabot bump)
3. Check vite 6 migration guide for breaking changes affecting our build (rollup version, plugin API, default behaviors)
4. Check vitest 4 migration guide (https://vitest.dev/guide/migration) — 9+ documented breaking changes; verify our config (vitest.config.ts) and test files don't use any removed APIs (basic reporter, ErrorWithDiff, getSourceMap, UserConfig from vitest's main entry)
5. Run pnpm exec tsc --noEmit + pnpm vitest run to confirm nothing breaks
6. Visual smoke after merge

While this is open, dependabot will keep re-proposing the vitest 4 bump every weekly scan. Options:
A) Add dependabot ignore rule for vitest majors in .github/dependabot.yml (and remove it as part of this upgrade PR)
B) Just close re-proposed PRs each week until this lands
C) Land this fix quickly enough that the noise is brief

Discovered: 2026-06-02 dependabot PR #274 triage.

title: Configure dependabot groups so react + react-dom + @types/react bump together (prevent tuxlink-ola6 recurrence)
description:
tuxlink-ola6 root cause: dependabot's react PR (#252) bumped 'react' from 19.2.6 → 19.2.7 + '@types/react' 19.2.15 → 19.2.16 WITHOUT bumping 'react-dom' (still 19.2.6). React 19 enforces strict version parity between react + react-dom; the mismatch threw an uncaught runtime exception at mount time, white-screening the entire app.

dependabot supports 'groups' config in .github/dependabot.yml that bundles related updates into a single PR. This task adds a 'react' group so the four packages always bump together.

Fix: edit .github/dependabot.yml, add to the existing npm config:

  groups:
    react:
      patterns:
        - 'react'
        - 'react-dom'
        - '@types/react'
        - '@types/react-dom'

Optionally add other peer-pair groups:
- '@radix-ui/*' (related radix packages tend to bump together)
- '@tauri-apps/*' (api + cli + plugin-shell typically pair)
- '@testing-library/*' (jest-dom + react)
- 'vitest + @vitest/*'

Verification: after merging, the next dependabot react PR should include all four package bumps in one PR instead of bumping piecemeal.

Discovered: 2026-06-02 after operator-reported white-screen regression (tuxlink-ola6 / PR #252).

title: [bug] ARDOP Radio section ALSA/PTT defaults are placeholder ghosts, not auto-fill: empty fields silently fail at ardopcf spawn
description: ArdopRadioPanel's Capture/Playback/PTT inputs render placeholder text (plughw:1,0 / /dev/ttyUSB0) that LOOKS auto-filled but is HTML placeholder ghosting only. The actual value is empty until the operator types something. commitCapture/commitPlayback persist empty strings; the ardopcf spawn then runs without -c/-p flags and either refuses to start or attaches to the system default device (rarely the radio). The PTT field maps empty -> null = VOX, which is a real choice but a poor default for HF where most operators want CAT-PTT. The WebGUI port input is the only field that TRULY auto-derives (cmd_port - 1 via resolveWebguiPort). Restore symmetry with the AX.25 picker (tuxlink-mqu3) by adding real device-enumeration backends: ardop_list_audio_devices (shells to arecord -L / aplay -L, parses the ALSA device list) + reuse packet_list_serial_devices for PTT. Frontend replaces the text-input ghosts with dropdown + Refresh + manual fallback, same pattern as ModemLinkSection.

title: VARA: send MYCALL after TCP open + emit session log lines on Start/Stop
description:
Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session) after PR #238 made Start clickable:
- VARA reports 'WARNING: VARA is not connected to any App via TCP Port 8300' because tuxlink opens the TCP socket but never sends MYCALL. VARA's host protocol expects MYCALL immediately as the identity handshake; without it, VARA considers the App as not properly connected.
- Radio modem pane's session log doesn't update on VARA Start/Stop because vara_start_session/vara_stop_session don't append to SessionLogState or emit session_log:line events. ARDOP/CMS backends use the emit_session_line helper pattern in ui_commands.rs; VARA needs equivalent.

Fix (one PR):
1. vara_start_session_inner: take callsign: Option<&str>; after TCP open, send MYCALL if Some. (Pre-MYCALL ordering: MYCALL is identity, BW is bandwidth, so MYCALL first.)
2. vara_start_session Tauri wrapper: read Config.identity.callsign, pass to _inner. Take AppHandle + SessionLogState state args; emit a session log line on Start (Info: 'VARA: opening transport...' on entry; Info: 'transport open' on success; Error: 'start failed — <msg>' on error).
3. vara_stop_session: same emit pattern (Info: 'VARA: transport closed').
4. Tests: existing _inner tests pass None for callsign; add a unit test verifying inner accepts callsign without panicking. Byte-on-wire MYCALL verification deferred to operator smoke (requires mock TCP server).

Pre-wizard (no callsign): skip MYCALL — VARA will continue to warn, but that's a wizard-completion problem, not a VARA bug.

bd: separate follow-up tuxlink-? filed for dashboard ribbon VARA integration (bigger surface).

title: Source segmented control: roving-tabindex + arrow-key keyboard navigation (WAI-ARIA APG radiogroup polish)
description:
tuxlink-z5pz segmented control review (commit 3598693 code-quality review) flagged that the radiogroup implementation does NOT follow the WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) for radiogroup keyboard navigation:

Current implementation:
- Both segments have role='radio' + aria-checked={...}
- Both segments are real <button> elements, so Tab navigates between them
- Space/Enter activates a segment

WAI-ARIA APG radiogroup pattern (deferred polish):
- Only the currently-checked radio is in the tab order (tabIndex=0); unchecked radios are tabIndex=-1
- Left/Right (or Up/Down) arrow keys move focus between radios in the group AND change selection
- Tab moves focus OUT of the radiogroup to the next focusable element

Spec §4.4 explicitly allows deferring this as P2 polish: 'Arrow keys could optionally implement radio-group keyboard navigation per WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices (left/right move focus between radios; activating with Space changes selection) — flag this as a P2 polish item if not implemented in this PR.'

This task implements the roving-tabindex + arrow-key handling. Scope:
- GridEdit.tsx: add tabIndex prop to each radio button (0 if checked, -1 otherwise)
- GridEdit.tsx: add onKeyDown handler to the radiogroup that intercepts ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/ArrowUp/ArrowDown and switches selection (firing onUseGps or onUseManual as appropriate)
- Tests: 4 new tests asserting (a) tab moves OUT of radiogroup, (b) ArrowRight on GPS when source=Manual fires onUseGps + focuses MANUAL, (c) ArrowLeft on MANUAL when source=Gps fires onUseManual + focuses GPS, (d) keyboard activation parity with click activation

This is a usability improvement for screen-reader + keyboard-only operators; not a correctness fix for any operator-reported defect.

title: MessageList sort UI — Phase 2 of mailbox-sort (operator-selectable sort with persistence)
description:
Fast-follow to tuxlink-mjc8 (PR #201 — backend-only deterministic ordering). Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session): 'Asking for a sort feature with no front end wiring/element would entirely defeat the point.' The backend sort gives a sensible default but the operator needs an interactive control.

Design: dropdown control rendered above the virtualized message list (MessageList rows are 3-line grids, not tabular — clickable column headers don't fit). Options:
- Newest first (default; matches the backend's date desc baseline)
- Oldest first
- Sender A→Z
- Sender Z→A
- Subject A→Z
- Subject Z→A

Sort applies client-side (sort the MessageMeta[] before passing to Virtuoso) so the backend's mailbox_list signature stays unchanged. Operator's choice persists in localStorage (global default; per-folder defaults can layer on later).

Implementation:
1. src/mailbox/messageSort.ts (new) — pure SortMode enum + compareMessages helper + tests
2. src/mailbox/MessageListSortControl.tsx (new) — small dropdown + tests
3. src/mailbox/MessageList.tsx — accept sortMode + onSortModeChange props, render control in a header row, sort array before Virtuoso
4. src/shell/AppShell.tsx — owns sortMode state + localStorage hydration/persistence
5. Test updates for any tests that pin pre-sort behavior

Phase 3 ideas (out of scope here): per-folder sort defaults, multi-key sort, unread-first.

title: VARA panel — ungate form fields on aarch64; tuxlink can connect to remote VARA over TCP
description:
Fast-follow to PR #221 (tuxlink-dfmf). Operator (2026-06-02, alder-gully-basalt session) found that on the Pi (aarch64), all form inputs (host/cmd_port/data_port/bandwidth) AND the Start button are disabled, with a red banner saying 'VARA requires an x86 / x86_64 host'.

That gating matches the original tuxlink-xfo spec ('show a disabled state with the explanation banner') but the spec was wrong. Pi can't RUN VARA locally (no Wine on aarch64), but Pi can absolutely CONNECT to a remote VARA over TCP — that's exactly what the 100.83.168.37:8300/8301 smoke target is for.

Fix:
1. Remove platformBlocked from the disabled={...} prop on host/cmd_port/data_port/bandwidth inputs and the Start button. loading || isOpen still apply.
2. Reword the banner from 'Controls below are disabled' → 'VARA cannot run *locally* on this host. Point host at a remote VARA instance to use it from here.'
3. Update VaraRadioPanel.test.tsx — the 'renders the Pi-availability banner on ARM and disables Start' test should now assert banner-presence + form-editability instead of Start-disabled.

Supersedes the inflexible gating intent in tuxlink-xfo (closable once this lands).

title: ARDOP panel elements jumbled / pushed off the right edge at 400px panel width
description: Operator-reported: ARDOP UI elements push past the right side of the radio panel even with the window maximized. Root cause is the textbook 1fr/flex:1 without min-width:0 companion pattern. SignalSection's Quality+SNR grid (104px 1fr) and RadioPanel's input rows (72px 1fr) resolve 1fr as minmax(auto, 1fr), so long content (ALSA device paths, PTT serial-by-id, etc.) pushes the track past the panel content box. FrameRibbon's flex:1 cells have the same defensive gap. Fix: swap to minmax(0, 1fr) and add min-width:0 to the shrinkable children. ModemLinkSection's repeat(3, 1fr) segmented picker has the same bug class — fix in the same PR.

title: VARA Phase 3 — session state machine + RF connect with RADIO-1 consent gate
description:
Phase 2 (tuxlink-dfmf) wired the minimal VARA TCP transport UI: open/close cmd+data sockets, edit host/ports/bandwidth, no RF transmission. Phase 3 adds:

1. Full VaraSession state machine — model VARA's INFO line + state events (CONNECTED <mycall> <target> [bw], PENDING, DISCONNECTED, PTT ON/OFF, BUFFER <n>, LINK REGISTERED, IAMALIVE, OFFLINE, error variants) as a session state mirror of ARDOP's ModemSession.

2. RF CONNECT path with RADIO-1 consent token (mirror modem_ardop_connect's mint→consume pattern):
   - vara_mint_consent (BACKEND mints, frontend never)
   - vara_connect_to_peer (consumes token atomically, sends CONNECT <mycall> <target>, manages the state transitions)
   - vara_disconnect_peer (sends DISCONNECT or ABORT depending on policy)

3. B2F-over-VARA integration — vara_b2f_exchange equivalent of modem_ardop_b2f_exchange. Reuse the b2f helper from ARDOP if possible (Phase 1's hblz issue raised the open question of whether to factor out a shared b2f.rs).

4. UI updates in VaraRadioPanel — Connect/Disconnect buttons + consent modal + peer-callsign input + connected-state display (peer + bw + buffer).

5. Bounded-airtime + abort discipline per feedback_radio1_bounded_airtime_abort.

Cross-refs:
- Phase 1 PR #199 (codec + transport)
- Phase 2 (this issue, tuxlink-dfmf) — to be merged before Phase 3 starts
- hblz issue: Phase 2 'awaits operator direction' design questions (Connect to peer state machine; b2f integration)
- v05-modem-design-posture memory: clean-room replacement is the long-term goal, but interim VARA support is the Phase 3 deliverable

title: Position subsystem CSS polish: .dimmed, .dash-set-manually, .dash-gps-no-fix-status, .dash-gps-ready-status
description:
Track A position-subsystem-restoration (tuxlink-c79g) referenced four CSS classes in JSX but didn't add CSS for them:

- .dash-gps-ready-status (T11 — passive GPS-ready hint span, State 2)
- .dimmed (T13 — dimmed chip modifier when source=Gps && !gpsReady, spec §2.4 row 4/5)
- .dash-set-manually (T13 — 'Set manually' button, State 4/5 affordance)
- .dash-gps-no-fix-status (T13 — GPS no fix status text)

T11/T13/T15 reviewers flagged this as deferred to a styling pass. Functionality + DOM contracts + tests are intact (tests assert by className substring + data-testid, not visual state). But the classes render without visual styling until CSS lands.

This task adds the CSS to AppShell.css (or wherever the dash- prefixed classes live) to give these elements their intended visual appearance per spec §2.4 / §4.4. Includes:
- .dimmed: reduced opacity for State 4/5 chip (e.g. opacity: 0.6, color shift to neutral)
- .dash-set-manually: link-like button styling consistent with other inline actions
- .dash-gps-ready-status: subtle status text styling (color + size matching other status indicators)
- .dash-gps-no-fix-status: warning-tinted status text

Operator smoke after CSS lands: walk through all 5 states (Manual+grid, Manual+nogrid, Gps+fix, Gps+nofix+grid, Gps+nofix+nogrid) in the running app and confirm each state's visual hierarchy is clear.

title: Convert useStatusData to react-query so T14 invalidateQueries actually triggers refetch
description:
Track A T14 added queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['config_read'] }) calls in DashboardRibbon after onCommit + onUseGps. However, useStatus.ts polls config_read via raw useState + useEffect + setInterval(load, 5000) — NOT via useQuery({ queryKey: ['config_read'], ... }). So the invalidation has no production refetch target; the chip + grid display still lag up to 5s after a user action.

To deliver the UX improvement the plan promised (chip flips within one render cycle), useStatusData needs to be refactored to use react-query with the ['config_read'] key. The T14 invalidation hook will then actually trigger refetch.

Scope:
- Refactor useStatusData in src/shell/useStatus.ts to use useQuery({ queryKey: ['config_read'], queryFn: () => invoke('config_read'), refetchInterval: 5000 }) and remove the setInterval pattern.
- Same for position_status if applicable.
- Verify no consumer regression (DashboardRibbon, GridEdit, status.test.ts).
- Re-run T14 tests — they should still pass (the spy on invalidateQueries still triggers).

Discovered: 2026-06-02 plover-willow-basalt while landing tuxlink-c79g T14.

title: [convergence-discipline] Add core.hooksPath bypass to destructive-git deny list
description: Per Codex 2026-06-01 P1 #3 (state-machine-hooks PR review): the ADR 0017 escape hatch is loud and audited. But an agent could trivially bypass the lifecycle hooks via git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit ... (silent and unaudited). Add to .claude/hooks/block-destructive-git.sh deny patterns: -c core.hooksPath= ; --config core.hooksPath= ; GIT_HOOKS_PATH= when in front of a git command. Same class as the existing skips-hooks pattern. The destructive-git hook is the right enforcement layer; ADR 0017's lifecycle hooks are not Claude-Code-aware and cannot police their own bypass surface. Parent: tuxlink-edvb (umbrella).

title: [convergence-discipline] converge-build v1 P2 polish: jq-safe audit JSON + gh-degradation provenance
description:
P2 follow-ups from the converge-build v1 Codex review (transcript at dev/adversarial/2026-06-01-converge-build-v1-codex.md on bd-tuxlink-qepd/converge-build). Both are robustness/forensics polish; the v1 PR addresses all P1 and P3 findings inline, defers these P2s.

Codex P2 #5 — Audit JSON not safe enough for failure forensics:
- audit() interpolates JSON strings directly via printf — could break if paths contain quotes/newlines.
- Untracked-collision logs flatten path arrays via IFS=',' (loses per-file SHAs).
- Process logs keep only PIDs, not cmdlines/cwds.
Change: Build audit lines with `jq -n --arg/--argjson` for path-bearing fields; include per-collision local+upstream SHAs; capture killed-process cmdline/cwd from /proc/<pid>/cmdline + cwd before sending SIGTERM.

Codex P2 #6 — Branch classification silently degrades if gh is unavailable/unauthenticated:
- classify_branch() falls through to category "bd-issue-active" with no warning if gh fails.
- A merged-dead branch on a host with no gh auth gets misclassified as active.
Change: Add gh_available + gh_error fields to the audit payload; warn on stderr when PR-state classification was unable to consult gh.

Parent: tuxlink-edvb (umbrella convergence-discipline)

title: [convergence-discipline] CI scheduled audit GitHub Action
description:
Codex P1 #9. Nightly GH Action that surveys all remote branches + classifies them per the state machine; flags orphan/dead-branch commits via a posted GH issue or PR comment. Catches non-Claude-tool merge paths (web UI, API merges, force pushes) that bypass the local hooks. Independent of converge-build's run-time check.

Parent: tuxlink-edvb (the umbrella convergence-discipline issue)

title: [convergence-discipline] Refactor converge-build to build from disposable worktree at origin/main
description:
Codex P1 #8. Currently v1 rebases task-amd-main-ui forward then builds from main checkout = builds from 'origin/main + handoff docs + restored stash overlay' which Codex called muddy SoT. Refactor to build from a persistent throwaway worktree (e.g. .local/converge-build-worktree/) checked out at exactly origin/main via 'git checkout --detach origin/main' (non-destructive). Cache node_modules + target across runs; wipe only on lockfile change. Handoff branch stays out of runtime build entirely.

Codex returned 2 P1, 2 P2, 1 P3, no P0 — all fixed inline (commit f6b5b57). Integration with dev-server lease (PR #206) deferred to stitch PR.

title: [convergence-discipline] Dev-server lease at ~/.config/tuxlink/dev-server.json
description:
Codex P1 #6. Replaces blind pkill-all-tauri-dev with a proper lease: file at ~/.config/tuxlink/dev-server.json records owning PID/cwd/branch/SHA. converge-build inspects the lease, displays 'currently held by X for Y minutes', requires explicit --force-kill-owned to terminate. Prevents disrupting concurrent agent work on parallel worktrees.

Integration with converge-build.sh deferred to stitch PR after #203 + #206 merge.

title: TCP P2P Telnet (Winlink) — design + implement client+listener
description:
Implement raw-TCP B2F P2P-Telnet to allow tuxlink to exchange mail with WLE on operator's Windows machine (configured as Telnet P2P listener or Post Office), using WLE's registered SID as the CMS-facing proxy. Addresses production-CMS SID-rejection blocker and enables real e2e smoke for the native Winlink client.

Scope:
- Client-dial transport (telnet_p2p.rs sibling to telnet.rs)
- P2P-listen transport (port bind + accept loop)
- Allowed-stations enforcement (callsigns + IPs, * wildcards)
- Optional station-password layer (keyring-stored, not INI-plaintext like WLE)
- UI surface: fills the unbuilt p2p+telnet cell in session-type-selector matrix (per docs/plans/2026-05-22-session-type-selector.md)

Two divergences from WLE parity (both improvements):
1. Allowed-stations default ENFORCED (vs WLE's permissive Allow All = TRUE default)
2. Station password in OS keyring (vs WLE's plaintext INI under callsign)

Design verified against WLE decompile findings at dev/scratch/winlink-re/findings/p2p-telnet.md (session larch-clover-delta, 2026-06-01). Design doc lands at docs/design/2026-06-01-tcp-p2p-telnet-design.md.

title: [P2] Fix nested {FormFolder} resolution in forms::http_server
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round finding: for templates in nested folders, {FormFolder} substitution percent-encodes the slash (e.g., Cat1%2FSub1) but axum decodes wildcard captures before reaching the handler's splitn. So a request for Cat1%2FSub1/file.css becomes Cat1/Sub1/file.css in rest, splitn(2, '/') gives folder_segment='Cat1' and file_path='Sub1/file.css', and the server looks under <template_folder>/Sub1/file.css instead of <template_folder>/file.css. The template_folder already pins to the right folder so the lookup is wrong twice over. Surface: src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:289-291. Fix sketch: drop the folder-segment parse entirely (the session is per-form, so the template folder is implicit) and treat the full wildcard as file_path relative to template_folder. Add a test fixture with nested folder.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-gheo-forms-http-server-hardening. Branch: bd-tuxlink-gheo/forms-http-server-hardening. Created 2026-06-01 17:58:40 UTC by bison-condor-grouse.

title: [P2] Add asset-size cap on forms::http_server /folder/<path>
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round finding: /folder/<path> requests in forms::http_server read the entire file into memory via std::fs::read (line ~311). A custom-form directory with a large image lets any local client allocate the entire file with no cap, exhausting memory on repeated requests. Fix sketch: (a) reject if file metadata.len() > 8 MB before read; OR (b) stream the file via tokio::fs::File + axum::body::Body::from_stream so memory pressure stays bounded. Option (b) is the more general fix but adds dep weight (axum-extra or tower-http streaming). Option (a) is simpler and fits the WLE-asset use case (form-adjacent images are small).

title: [P2] Bound the forms::http_server submission channel
description: Codex 2026-06-01 P1 round on tuxlink-ytya/p1-webview-infra finding: while a session is open, any local client (with origin spoofed past the new origin check, or pre-fix) can send many valid POSTs and each parsed body is queued into an unbounded mpsc channel. The 1 MB cap limits one request but not total queued memory or duplicate submissions. A bounded/one-shot channel with backpressure would prevent a local flood from growing memory until the compose receiver catches up or closes. Surface: src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:122 (the mpsc::unbounded_channel() call in FormSession::open). Fix sketch: switch to mpsc::channel(1) (one in-flight submission per session — multiple submits in a single session is anomalous anyway, since the form closes itself after submit per the WLE convention). Update submit_handler to await send().await with a timeout; on full, drop with a 503 response.

title: Wire up VARA TCP transport (HF/VHF) — mirror ARDOP ModemTransport pattern
description: Implement a VARA TCP transport in src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/ as a sibling to the existing ardop/ module, implementing the same ModemTransport trait. VARA TCP exposes two sockets: command (default 8300) ASCII \r-terminated, and data (default 8301) raw byte stream. Common commands: MYCALL <call>, BW <bw>, LISTEN ON/OFF, CONNECT <mycall> <target>, DISCONNECT, ABORT, BUFFER. Responses: CONNECTED/DISCONNECTED/PTT/PENDING/BUFFER/IAMALIVE. Smoke target: 100.83.168.37:8300 (cmd) / 8301 (data) — operator-hosted test instance with no radio attached, so no RADIO-1 transmission concerns. Mirror ardop's file layout: command.rs (commands), wire.rs (parse/emit), session.rs (high-level types), transport.rs (TCP I/O), mod.rs (config + ModemTransport impl). Operator-requested overnight task — bookmark for review next session.
notes:
Phase 1 status (2026-06-01, larch-clover-delta session):

✅ Smoke gate met (probe round-trips against 100.83.168.37 cleanly)
✅ Codec extended with BusyDetected(bool), CommandAck, Registered variants (folded into PR #192 since smoke was PR-scope)
✅ tuxlink-efw2 filed + closed in same commit
✅ PR #192 marked ready for review (was draft)
✅ 23 vara module tests pass; clippy clean on lib + vara_tcp_probe bin
✅ Smoke probe re-run shows zero Unknown(verbatim) events — all banner+ack+status lines now typed

Phase 2 awaits operator direction:
- Pattern reference: ardop/transport.rs (2101 lines) + b2f.rs (460 lines) on main
- Open design questions before Phase 2 code starts:
  1. Mirror ARDOP state machine exactly, or adapt for VARA's different banner/handshake?
  2. How to integrate VARA's per-command OK ack pattern into the ModemTransport trait (which has no "wait for ack" primitive)?
  3. B2F integration: clone ARDOP's b2f.rs or factor out a shared helper?
  4. Per [[v05-modem-design-posture]] (technical merit over compatibility), is there an opportunity for cleaner abstractions vs. ARDOP-clone?

Phase 1 scope (PR #192) is the natural v0.1 codec ship. Phase 2 = subsequent PR.

title: Compose chrome label collisions with form-body labels: To/From/Subject appear in both surfaces
description:
UX issue surfaced during operator browser smoke 2026-06-01: the compose window's chrome has top-level 'To', 'From', 'Subject' inputs (which address the email envelope — recipient callsign(s), originator callsign, message subject). The ICS-213 (and likely future) form components also have 'To (Name and Position)', 'From (Name and Position)', and 'Subject' fields inside the form body (which are operator names + a form-internal subject distinct from the message subject). Operators new to the flow fill the chrome's To and assume the form's To is duplicate; the form's Send button stays disabled because to_name/fm_name/subjectline are unfilled with no clear cause.

Proposed brainstorm:
- Option A: rename form labels in React to disambiguate (e.g., 'To: addressee name', 'From: originator name', 'Form subject (distinct from message subject)'). Wire-format field IDs (to_name, fm_name, subjectline) stay unchanged for WLE/Pat compat.
- Option B: visually group the chrome inputs vs the form inputs more clearly (section borders + headings like 'Envelope' vs 'ICS-213 form').
- Option C: pre-populate form fields from chrome where logical (form's 'From' could pre-fill from chrome's callsign, etc.)

Operator-decision needed before implementation.

title: Floor rate-1/4 LDPC needs PEG (or column-swap-pivot) construction for rank-full H
description: PR for tuxlink-bbin (subsystem #4 FEC) Phase 4 lands a working systematic LDPC encoder for the WiFi-family codes (n=648, n=1296 at rates 1/2-5/6) via seed-iteration on rank-deficiency. The floor rate-1/4 (n=2048, k=512, m=1536) configuration-model construction does not produce rank-full H matrices reliably — 64 seed iterations exhaust without finding a rank-full instance. This is a known property of short-block random regular (3,4) LDPC where the m × m right-half submatrix has substantial rank-deficiency probability under structured row/column weight constraints. The fix is one of: (a) PEG (progressive-edge-growth, Hu-Eleftheriou-Arnold 2005) construction that guarantees full-rank generator; (b) column-swap pivoting in the encoder (record a column permutation, encode under permuted H, present systematic-form codeword on the external API). The Phase 4 encoder_handles_floor_rate14 test is currently #[ignore]'d. Un-ignore once one of (a) or (b) lands. This is the gate for #3's wideband_floor_vs_ardop_target test as well.

title: Implement subsystem #4 FEC (tuxmodem-fec v0.1)

title: Author missing CSS for per-form authoring + view components
description:
While fixing the FormPicker CSS gap (commit 4451d27, PR #177), audited
all classNames in src/forms/**/*.tsx and confirmed that 17 of them have
zero CSS rules anywhere in the project:

PER-FORM AUTHORING (compose-side):
- .ics213-form, .ics309-form, .bulletin-form, .damage-assessment-form, .position-form
- .ics309-log-section, .ics309-log-entry, .ics309-log-table
- .damage-category, .damage-category-table, .damage-comments, .damage-total-cost

VIEW (read-side, inbound message detail):
- .form-view-header, .form-view-exercise-marker, .form-message-body
- .form-fields (the <dl> of label/value pairs in each *View.tsx)

SHARED:
- .form-actions (button row, used by every Form component)
- .form-field-row (referenced but origin unclear — audit which form uses it)

WHY THIS WASN'T CAUGHT EARLIER: vitest + jsdom don't load CSS, so the
component tests pass green regardless. The fragility was hidden until
operator browser-smoke. The FormPicker.css fix is the visible-blocker
piece; the rest of these only become visible once the operator clicks
through to a form. See pitfall TEST-1 in PR #179 (already covers part of
this lesson) — possible extension noted in v1p fix commit message.

SCOPE NOTES:
- Mirror the .compose-* token vocabulary already in Compose.css
  (--tux-surface, --tux-border, --tux-accent, etc.; see App.css:75-100
  for the full palette).
- The .form-fields <dl> wants a 2-column grid (dt/dd pairs).
- ICS-309 and Damage Assessment have table-style log/category grids
  that need real table styling; the rest are simpler vertical-stack forms.
- Decide whether to author per-form CSS files imported from each form
  component, or one shared src/forms/forms.css side-effect-imported from
  src/forms/index.ts.

VERIFICATION: Browser-smoke each form (compose-side AND inbound render
via DEV-3 fixtures) before declaring done. Vitest cannot certify visual
correctness here.

title: ARDOP: parse PINGACK + PING events for structured sn_db (deferred from PR #162)
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) found that PR #162 deferred sn_db because we assumed STATUS-string parsing was the only source. Wrong: ardopcf has structured PINGACK and PING events that carry S/N + quality as explicit numeric fields.

ardopcf events (Host_Interface_Commands.md):
- PINGACK SNdB Quality — reply to an outbound PING
- PING caller>target SNdB Quality — incoming ping

Fix (~50 LOC):
1. Add PingAck { sn_db: i32, quality: u32 } and possibly PingReply { caller, target, sn_db, quality } variants to Command enum (command.rs).
2. Extend Command::parse to handle them.
3. In apply_event_to_accumulators_inline, on PingAck → status.sn_db = Some(sn as f32).
4. For the operator to GET a PingAck, they fire an explicit PING command. Options:
   (a) New 'Ping' button in dock that sends PING <target>.
   (b) Automatic PING right before Connect (single shot to populate S/N before handshake).
5. Add Tauri command modem_ardop_ping(target) wrapping cmd.send_line('PING <target>').
6. Tests: stub transport emits PINGACK event; verify status.sn_db updates.

Unblocks the deferred numeric meter from PR #162.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP: expose ARQBW bandwidth selection (200/500/1000/2000 Hz) in Settings + InitConfig
description:
The 2026-05-31 ARDOP gap audit (dev/scratch/2026-05-31-ardop-gap-audit.md) identified bandwidth selection as a P1 operator gap. ardopcf supports the ARQBW host command with values 200/500/1000/2000 Hz; the value can be FORCED by the client or negotiated with the server.

Today: ARQBW is already in our setter whitelist (command.rs:is_setter_echo_back) but never wired through. ardopcf uses whatever default is. Operators on a marginal HF path can't drop to 500 Hz; operators with clean conditions can't push to 2000 Hz.

Fix (~30 LOC):
1. Add bandwidth_hz: Option<u32> to ArdopUiConfig (Settings panel field).
2. Add bandwidth_hz: Option<u32> to InitConfig (session.rs).
3. In init_tnc, after LISTEN FALSE, send ARQBW <hz> FORCED if config set.
4. Surface as a Settings dropdown: Auto (default) / 200 / 500 / 1000 / 2000 Hz.

Validates against AE8Q operator walkthrough (https://ae8q.com/2022/03/winlink-hf-ardop-howto/) which says 'Width: 2000, 1000, 500' is operator-selectable. Smoke-blocker if a first connect fails on a marginal path.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP HF UI: frontend Send/Receive button wires modem_ardop_b2f_exchange (post #159)
description:
PR #159 shipped the backend B2F-over-ARDOP path (modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command) but no UI button calls it. Today the operator must invoke from the WebView console:

  const tok = await __TAURI__.invoke('modem_mint_consent');
  await __TAURI__.invoke('modem_ardop_b2f_exchange', { target: 'W7RMS-10', consentToken: tok });

For the dial to actually deliver mail in the dock UX, add a 'Send/Receive' (or similar) button to ArdopDock that:

1. Reads the configured CMS target from connect config (or accepts a target field — likely the same target the operator dialed in the Connect form).
2. Mints a fresh consent token via modem_mint_consent.
3. Invokes modem_ardop_b2f_exchange.
4. Surfaces progress/errors in the dock (the backend's ProgressSink already emits Transport-tagged session log lines; the dock might also show a simple 'B2F exchange in progress…' state).
5. On completion, refreshes the mailbox view so any received mail appears.

Probably small (~2-4 hours): mostly wiring + a few tests. The backend is done; this is the UX bridge.

Closes the 'ARDOP HF as a real feature' gap noted in PR #159's body.

title: ARDOP HF: extend ModemStatusBroadcaster to poll ardopcf cmd-socket for live meters
description:
The Task 3.4 ModemStatusBroadcaster (introduced by PR #153) emits modem:status every 250ms but only rebroadcasts the cached snapshot — there's no live polling of ardopcf's cmd-socket. Result: the dock renders the meter UI (S/N, VU input, Throughput, bytes RX/TX, uptime, ARQ state grid) but the values never update with real signal-quality data; they stay at whatever modem_ardop_connect set at connect time.

Plan noted this deferral explicitly: 'For v1 the broadcaster just emits the cached snapshot; the richer flow (poll the ardopcf cmd-socket for live S/N, throughput, etc.) is filed as a follow-up bd issue once the dock ships.' I forgot to file the follow-up at session end (2026-05-30 kite-larch-kingfisher); operator surfaced the gap during PR #153 review.

Work:
1. In ModemStatusBroadcaster::spawn (modem_status.rs), don't just snapshot — also drain pending events from the ardopcf CmdSocket via session.try_recv_event() (or similar non-blocking accessor on the installed transport).
2. Map ardopcf events to ModemStatus fields:
   - STATE → ModemState transitions (Connecting/ConnectedIrs/ConnectedIss/Disconnecting/Idle)
   - BUFFER → bytes_rx / bytes_tx + throughput_bps (rolling avg)
   - MYCALL / TARGET → peer
   - PINGREP / PING → sn_db
   - DRIVELEVEL / INPUTPEAKS → vu_dbfs (per ardopcf docs)
   - ARQ_BUSY / ARQ_RX / ARQ_TX → arq_flags
3. Write the updated ModemStatus back via session.set_status(...) (single lock acq) — the existing broadcaster snapshot loop already picks up the change on its next tick.
4. Test plan:
   - Unit: stub a CmdSocket that emits canned events; assert ModemStatus transitions.
   - Operator smoke: connect to a gateway, watch the dock's meters animate during a B2F exchange.

Blocks: 'ARDOP HF as a real feature' depends on this. The dock is useless to the operator if meters never update.

Adjacent: tuxlink-ytg (ARDOP data path + B2F wiring) is parallel — that's the mail-flow side; this is the visibility side. Both needed for a real ARDOP HF v1.

title: v0.1 capability 1.15: find-messages (FTS5 over filesystem mailbox + filters + saved searches)
description:
v0.1 message-action capability 1.15 from the Winlink Express feature inventory
(docs/design/2026-05-29-winlink-express-feature-inventory.md §1.15).

Tuxlink is greenfield, so this isn't bounded to Express's grep-headers floor:
ship floor + structured filter chips + saved searches, indexed via sqlite
FTS5 in a derived companion db (mailbox stays filesystem-canonical per
native_mailbox.rs).

Brainstorm in progress (session finch-gulch-lichen, resuming fen-alder-bog).
Q1–Q3 settled per dev/handoffs/2026-05-30-fen-alder-bog-find-messages-brainstorm-paused.md:

- Q1: floor + structured filters + saved searches.
- Q2: index headers + body + form-payload fields (PDF/attachment text deferred to v0.5+).
- Q3: sqlite FTS5 in derived search.db alongside filesystem mailbox.

Q4 (filter chip set: lean / medium-recommended / max+tags) is the next
clarifying question. Q5 (saved-searches model), Q6 (UI placement), Q7 (sync /
re-index strategy) follow.

Deliverables (this issue covers design + implementation):

description:

Produce a comprehensive inventory document at docs/design/<date>-winlink-express-feature-inventory.md that enumerates every Winlink Express feature visible through menus and buttons, grouped by functional area (mailbox, compose, session/connect, settings/properties, tools, help, etc.), with for each:

  - Where it lives (menu path / which Form)
  - Brief description of what it does
  - EmComm relevance / frequency-of-use signal (operator decides)
  - Current Tuxlink status: shipped, planned, deferred, intentionally-dropped
  - If dropped/deferred: the rationale (clean-sheet rebuild, Linux-native equivalent, Pat/native split, etc.)

The goal: make every omission a RATIONALIZED design choice, not an accidental gap. Operator (Cameron) reviews the inventory and decides per-feature. Per feedback_winlink_re_authoritative_sources, decompiled RMS Express is endorsed prior-art ground truth for this work. (Note: VARA modem internals remain out of scope per project_v05_modem_design_posture; this audit is the email-client UI surface, not the modem.)

title: ARDOP data path: coordinate cmd connection-state with DataSocket (EOF-on-DISCONNECT + gate ARQ-before-connect) — wire with B2F
description: Codex adversarial review of tuxlink-6aj (commit 582bbe1) found two related B2F-integration issues in the ARDOP data path that only bite once the sync B2F run_exchange is actually wired to DataSocket (deferred; out of MVP scope): (1) DataSocket::read returns EOF only when the data TCP socket closes, but ardopcf keeps the data socket OPEN and reports link drop via CMD-socket events (DISCONNECTED / NEWSTATE DISC). A sync B2F read_until on the data stream can therefore HANG forever after an on-air disconnect. (2) The data socket is opened at init (before ARQ connect) and DataSocket::read accepts EVERY ARQ-tagged frame; wl2k-go drops ARQ frames while not connected because ARDOPc can emit monitored / non-session data as ARQ — so stale RF data could be fed into the first B2F handshake read. Both need the SAME mechanism: the data path must observe the cmd-socket connection state. FIX when wiring B2F: share a connected/disconnected signal (an AtomicBool or a socket shutdown handle) from the CmdSocket control loop to the DataSocket so (a) a DISCONNECT unblocks / EOFs the data read, and (b) inbound ARQ frames are ignored until connected. Also consider the conn.go outbound flow-control refinement (Write waits for a BUFFER update / retries on CRCFault) for throughput. Reference: wl2k-go conn.go (Read pulls from a dataIn channel the control loop CLOSES on disconnect; tnc.go eof()). Blocks the B2F-over-ARDOP wiring (future phase).
notes: PR #157 opened. Backend wiring complete: ArqState shared atomic between cmd+data sockets, b2f.rs exchange driver, modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command, EOF-on-DISC + pre-connect frame drop + ARQ-before-write gate all implemented. 422 lib tests green, clippy clean. Frontend Send/Receive button wiring is the follow-up PR.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #159 (merge 5df177d) — run_ardop_b2f_exchange + modem_ardop_b2f_exchange Tauri command + Codex P1 fixes (FAULT→ArqState, DataSocket::drain_pending). Dep tuxlink-6aj shipped as PR #138 in v0.2; closing past the stale dep edge.

title: Add ARDOP HF transport (managed-spawn; generic external-TCP-modem client)
description:
LOCKED 2026-05-24 (operator). Add ARDOP HF support so operators already using ARDOP are not cut off (tuxlink is a tool, not a vanity project).

ARCHITECTURE LOCKED:
- tuxlink LAUNCHES + OWNS the modem lifecycle (managed-spawn): tuxlink is the single arbiter of the one-sound-card conflict — spins Dire Wolf down / ardopcf up on a VHF<->HF switch; SIGINT clean-stop (ardopcf ignores SIGHUP); confirm the audio device is RELEASED before the swap.
- The ARDOP transport is a GENERIC external-TCP-modem client, NOT ARDOP-special-cased: ardopcf / Dire Wolf / VARA / (future) first-party tuxmodem are all instances of ONE transport abstraction (drive a modem over its TCP host protocol + manage its process).

Target modem: ardopcf (LaRue fork). NOT in apt -> bundle as a Tauri sidecar (src-tauri/sidecars/ exists; confirm per-arch triples + license). Host interface: TCP 8515 cmd + 8516 data (+ optional 8514 webgui for level tuning). Target ARDOP v1/ardopcf (v2 abandoned). Audio: ardopcf needs 12 kHz -> ALSA plughw: (resampling) DIRECT to the radio USB codec; avoid PipeWire/Pulse routing for ARQ (Pi bookworm defaults to PipeWire — keep it off the device).

STILL OPEN: PTT/frequency sequencing — MVP (ardopcf -p RTS PTT, G90-proven; operator sets HF dial freq manually; NO rig-control dependency) vs FULL single-pane (tuxlink owns Hamlib CAT+PTT via the tuxlink-5jb rig-control crate). MVP can ship first; FULL depends on tuxlink-5jb.

PRE-PLAN: research locked, NOT an implementation plan — do not start coding before a plan / modem ADR. Full findings: docs/design/ardop-deployment-findings.md.
notes: MVP MERGED to main as PR #138 (c08bd8c base at merge time). Code complete: ADR 0015, winlink::modem::ardop wire/session/data/transport, ModemTransport, ManagedModem (SIGINT/SIGKILL+lsof), examples/ardop_connect CLI. Codex P0 (outbound framing) + 3 P1/P2 fixed before merge; 366 lib tests green. RADIO-1: on-air NOT validated — operator on-site via examples/ardop_connect. Follow-ups still tracked: tuxlink-ig0 (bind-wait passive check), tuxlink-ytg (data-path cmd-state for B2F), tuxlink-5jb (rig-control crate). KEEP OPEN until on-air bring-up + B2F wiring complete.

title: Re-evaluate AX.25 Bluetooth transport: revert nx2 socket pivot to proven serialport-TTY (keep 2y4 safety) — needs on-site on-air test
description:
Operator (RF authority) flagged that tearing down the working Bluetooth packet implementation to chase an unconfirmed double-TX may have been premature. Remote investigation (agent marten-finch-gorge, 2026-05-24) confirms the concern and pinpoints the real culprit.

WHAT THE TEARDOWN ACTUALLY WAS — two conflated changes:
(A) nx2 transport pivot (commits a511c69 + e123da9): swapped the PROVEN-RX serialport-TTY Bluetooth path (/dev/rfcommN, the transport that got the on-air W7MOT-6 DM) for an UNPROVEN-RX raw AF_BLUETOOTH/BTPROTO_RFCOMM socket, on a termios-teardown theory the author admits was never proven (raven-salamander-tamarack Honest reframe). Suspected root of tuxlink-4ef (TX works, RX broken). THIS is the premature teardown.
(B) 2y4 safety bundle (commit ea8ac94): NOT the questionable teardown. Five separable fixes; four are zero-cost to keep (abort-before-TX in write(), no pre-connect DISC, KISS decoder accepts any-port data frame, and the revert of the wrong-lever uhc T1 floor). The fifth is a tunable max-2-SABM + 25 s connect airtime cap. 2y4 ALREADY reverted the uhc T1 floor that was the actual double-TX fix the operator objected to.

KEY MECHANISM FINDING: the baseline connect() (OTA-reply state, a0838a5) keyed a SABM every T1 up to n2_retries+1 = 11 times (for _attempt in 0..=params.n2_retries). At default T1=3 s against a gateway that answered the DM at ~7 s, that fires SABMs at 0/3/6 s = three keyings in fast succession before the reply = the most likely mechanism of the double-key the operator observed on-air. So the double-key was REAL (operator-observed), but the RESPONSE to it (uhc T1 floor 10 s -> ~110 s runaway observed on a wrong-worktree build -> transport pivot) was the over-reaction. The 2y4 bounded-airtime fix (max 2 SABMs then listen) is proportionate and keys FEWER times than the baseline.

RECOMMENDED ACTION (when physically present): on a branch off main/consolidation HEAD, revert the nx2 transport routing so the Bluetooth link uses the serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) path again (proven RX); KEEP all of 2y4. Then run one bounded + abortable + instrumented on-air dial (RADIO-1, operator-keyed) to confirm RX is restored and the rapid double-key is gone.

WHY DEFERRED: operator is fully remote over Tailscale and cannot key the radio or observe on-air results. Potentially-breaking transport changes without on-site follow-up testing do not advance the project and risk confusing future troubleshooting. Execute only when physically present at pandora.

GATE: blocked on tuxlink-9ky (Pi-side Bluetooth Page-Timeout) — no Bluetooth transport of any kind can be tested until the Pi can page the UV-Pro.
design:
Change surface for the transport revert:
- src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/link.rs — KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth { mac } routing (added by nx2); route Bluetooth back to the serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) open path (commit 4226299 connect_link serial arm).
- src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/rfcomm.rs — the in-app RfcommSocket (nx2); stop using / remove. NOTE ad79492 (4ef byte trace) also edits rfcomm.rs — handle that overlap.
- connect_link dispatch + the TS DTO btMac (e123da9) + the UI USB/BT device picker (must surface /dev/rfcommN again; tuxlink-sox notes /dev/rfcomm0 did not appear in the picker — discovery gap to fix alongside).
nx2 commits to reverse: a511c69, e123da9 (+ the trace bits in ad79492 touching the socket). Use additive reverts, not history rewinds.
KEEP entirely: ea8ac94 (2y4). Its fixes live at the datalink/AbortableByteLink layer and are transport-agnostic, so they apply equally to the serialport-TTY path.
Mechanism constraint: history-rewind is banned (CLAUDE.md destructive-git ban) and the main checkout is operator state — do this in a worktree on a new branch (bd-ID/slug), never by rewinding a branch.
RELATED: candidate resolution for tuxlink-4ef (RX broken); relates to tuxlink-uhc (double-key), tuxlink-nx2 (the socket), tuxlink-sox (transport persistence + picker gap).
acceptance_criteria: Serialport-TTY (/dev/rfcommN) restored as the active Bluetooth transport; all of 2y4 retained; lib + FE test suites green. On-site bounded+abortable on-air dial (operator-keyed, RADIO-1) shows: two-way exchange (RX restored), no rapid double-key on connect, and a working Cancel that halts TX.
notes: Confirmed this session (2026-05-24): Dire Wolf 1.7 (/usr/bin/direwolf) provides a WORKING TCP/IP software TNC — AGW protocol on :8000 and KISS-over-TCP on :8001, both verified accepting + attaching a TCP client using ADEVICE null (no soundcard, no radio). Bluetooth-free transport option for transport-layer testing (tuxlink -> TCP KISS/AGW -> Dire Wolf -> soundcard -> radio), independent of the BT Page-Timeout. soundmodem (Sailer Linux build) NOT installed; Dire Wolf covers the role. Test config: dev/scratch/dw-tcp-test.conf.

title: Config-read resilience: a divergent packet.link variant (or unknown field) bricks app-open instead of degrading
description: A parallel branch (tuxlink-nx2, uhc/ax25-tx-timing) added KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth{mac}; its build wrote packet.link={"Bluetooth":...} to the SHARED ~/.config/tuxlink/config.json. Builds without that variant (main, #124/tuxlink-3pb) hard-fail on open: KissLinkConfig is externally-tagged (unknown variant rejected, no serde(other) fallback) and Config is deny_unknown_fields. read_config() returns Err and the app bricks. NOTE: read_config's documented wizard consumers (.ok() -> fresh wizard) degrade gracefully, but SOME startup path propagates the error as fatal — that propagating consumer is the fix locus (degrade to None/wizard, don't brick). OWNER: the nx2/Bluetooth work should ship read-tolerance so merging it + version-skew don't brick configs; coordinate (bd dep) rather than two sessions editing config.rs/link.rs at once. DEV HAZARD (generalizable): concurrent branch builds sharing one config + deny_unknown_fields = cross-contamination bricking (config-file cousin of the worktree :1420 collision). Mitigations: graceful read degradation on schema divergence AND/OR per-worktree TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR for in-dev builds. Found during #124 (tuxlink-3pb) browser smoke 2026-05-22.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: AX.25 Path::encode hardcodes C/R=command for ALL frames — responses (UA/RR-as-response) mis-framed per v2.2
description: frame.rs Path::encode (~line 224) always sets dest C-bit=1 / src C-bit=0 with comment 'cr=true for a command frame; refined in P2' — never refined. CORRECT for commands (SABM/DISC/I-frames), but WRONG for RESPONSES: a UA sent when ANSWERING an inbound call, and RR/RNR/REJ sent as responses to a poll, must per AX.25 v2.2 be dest C-bit=0 / src C-bit=1. A strict v2.2 decoder (RMS gateway, BPQ, Direwolf) may reject/mis-handle our responses. This is the independent-decoder-visible class of bug that peer-to-peer + TCP-relay testing (shared codec) could never catch — surfaced now that we're on-air against a real gateway. NOT the cause of the 2026-05-22 SABM-dial DM (a SABM IS a command and is correctly framed; address shift, SSID reserved/ext bits, control 0x3F, KISS port-0 wrap, and no-FCS-for-KISS all verified spec-correct). Fix: thread command/response through Path::encode (or Frame::encode) so response frames set the C/R bits correctly. Most impactful on the LISTEN/answer path (tuxlink-as-master sends UA) + sustained exchanges (RR acks).
notes: Reopened 2026-06-02 (taiga-arroyo-clover): PR #228 closed by operator — AX.25 C/R bit changes touch on-air TX bytes and need verification against a real v2.2 gateway. Out of scope for this session (focus was telnet/UI). Re-land after on-air verification.

title: Packet panel: transport segment resets to USB Serial instead of persisting last-selected (Bluetooth)
description: On-air 2026-05-22: the packet connect panel's transport segment 'constantly resets to USB Serial' instead of staying on Bluetooth (the last-selected mode). Likely cause (verify, do not assume): USB and Bluetooth BOTH map to linkKind:'Serial' (per spec/code 'USB or Bluetooth-RFCOMM both use kind:serial'), so when the panel re-derives the segment from config on reload/poll it can't distinguish BT from USB and defaults to USB. Fix: persist/derive the BT-vs-USB segment distinctly (stored sub-kind, or infer from device path /dev/rfcommN vs /dev/ttyUSB*). Frontend src/packet/PacketConnectionPanel.tsx. Related: the bound /dev/rfcomm0 also did NOT appear in the BT device picker (packet_list_serial_devices discovery gap) — operator had to type the path.

title: Packet AX.25: radio double-keys on connect; configured T1/TXdelay/KISS params not respected
description: On-air 2026-05-22 (UV-Pro, ch5, dial W7MOT-6): the connect caused the radio to key TWICE in very fast succession (operator: 'not good'), and the operator (RF-expert) reports the default AX.25 parameters (T1/TXdelay/persistence/slot) are NOT being honored. INVESTIGATE (do not assume a cause): (1) config->Ax25Params mapping (PacketConfig into_params) — are the configured T1/timing values actually used, or defaults? (2) kiss_param frame correctness — the KISS command nibble MUST be 1-6 (config: TXDELAY=1/P=2/SlotTime=3/...); if any is 0 (data) it would TX the payload and cause spurious keying. (3) connect() T1 retransmit loop (datalink.rs) — a too-short T1 vs gateway RF latency fires a 2nd SABM before the 1st response arrives. The connect reached the gateway (DM back) so the transport works; this is RF-politeness/timing. Needs careful look, not a session-end guess. Evidence: session log 01:41:52 connect -> 01:41:59 DM.
notes: SUPERSEDED-IN-PART by tuxlink-2y4: the T1 floor (PR #123) is to be REVERTED; the real fix (bounded airtime + abort + no pre-connect DISC) is 2y4. The double-key was one symptom of a broader unsafe connect envelope.

title: Validate packet B2F against an INDEPENDENT KISS decoder (Dire Wolf / kernel AX.25), no RF
description: The tuxlink-3wh end-to-end proof wires two tuxlink peers together, which exercises the whole stack but SHARES the codec — a symmetric framing/FCS bug would be invisible. Validate KISS+AX.25 interop against an independent implementation with no RF: e.g. Dire Wolf as a software KISS TNC + the Linux kernel AX.25 stack (kissattach) as the connected-mode peer, on localhost/audio-loopback. Needs operator OK for 'sudo apt install direwolf ax25-tools' (per no-unilateral-apt rule). Catches codec bugs the peer-to-peer test cannot. Ride on tuxlink-7fr.

title: Packet: real in-app Bluetooth/USB TNC connect flow (pairing/binding support)
description: tuxlink does NOT meaningfully connect a Bluetooth TNC: the hard part (bluetoothctl pair + rfcomm bind /dev/rfcommN, needs sudo, deprecated on modern BlueZ) is unsupported manual OS work; the app only scans /dev for an already-bound node + a one-line hint (spec 4.1 punts pairing to the OS). serialBaud is meaningless for RFCOMM but still passed to serialport::open() (untested). Decide + build: either in-app BT discover/pair/bind, or at minimum a guided setup flow; verify the serial open path on real USB + BT hardware. Same applies to USB device-open (never run on hardware).

title: Packet: live connect/listen status feed (backend events -> ribbon/panel)
description: The ribbon/status bar + panel show only static state ('Packet 1200 - not connected') and the Listen control's 'armed' is purely frontend-local, because there is no backend->frontend feed of real connect/listen state. Wire backend status/events (Connecting/Connected/Listening-armed/Answering <call>/Disconnected) so the UI reflects what the link is actually doing. Honest-static was the interim fix; this is the real version.
notes: BUILT + GATED (chasm-sorrel-glade, 2026-05-22, commit 0aa1002 on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet): honest live status feed landed. BackendStatus::Listening variant (armed-but-idle, distinct from Connecting); initial_packet_status maps Listen->Listening / DialTo->Connecting (pure, unit-tested); frontend StatusDto union + formatConnectionState + formatStatusState gain Listening, normalizeTransportLabel renders 'Packet 1200'; derivePacketUiState wires the live backend_status poll into the packet indicator, replacing the hard-coded 'not connected' placeholder (honest by construction). Gates: cargo --lib 306 ok, vitest 419 ok, tsc clean. Left IN_PROGRESS pending operator app-smoke of the live indicator (UI-visible feed; real proof is seeing it in the running app + on-air) — same posture as tuxlink-3wh. Real-peer-in-Connected + 'Answering <call>' transient split to a new on-air-only follow-up (status sink in native_packet_connect).

title: ax25: clean Stop for a serial/Bluetooth packet listen (abort over serialport)
description: packet Listen Stop works over TCP (cms_abort shuts the cloned TcpStream -> recv_frame ConnectionAborted -> answer() unwinds). For a USB/BT serial KISS TNC, connect_link_with_abort returns None (no try_clone equivalent for a serialport handle), so abort() is a no-op on the link and a waiting answer() can't be stopped except by disconnecting the TNC. Add a serial abort mechanism: e.g. a stop-flag the answer poll loop checks, or a serialport break/close handle. Built 2026-05-22 on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet.
notes: BUILT + GATED (chasm-sorrel-glade, 2026-05-22, commit 12486ff on bd-tuxlink-nj1/serial-stop, PR #119). Serial/BT packet Listen Stop now works: AbortableByteLink (link.rs) wraps the serial link and returns ConnectionAborted once the backend's existing 'aborting' flag is set; recv_frame already maps that to an abort, so answer()/connect() unwind on the next poll (bounded by the 200ms serial read timeout). connect_link_with_abort takes the abort Arc<AtomicBool>; TCP keeps its immediate socket-shutdown fast path (flag unused there). native_packet_connect threads the existing aborting flag in (no new state). Hardware-free tests: AbortableByteLink flag->ConnectionAborted read; answer() unwinds with ConnectionAborted on an aborting link. cargo --lib 311 ok. Left IN_PROGRESS pending OPERATOR on-air verification (a real Stop against a serial/BT TNC e.g. UV-Pro /dev/rfcomm0 — RADIO-1).

'cargo test' on origin/main fails to compile these test targets (E0063 missing field position_source) — CI must not be compiling them (cf. tuxlink-b2s CI path-filter gap). Fixed on bd-tuxlink-7fr/ax25-packet (will propagate when it merges); flagging so trunk is fixed independently and the CI gap that let it through is addressed.

title: ax25: resolve Ax25Stream::read() Ok(0)-vs-EOF contract when P4 wires run_exchange
description: P2 review (opus EXTRA-1 + Codex #9): Ax25Stream::read returns Ok(0) for 'no data yet' (link open), which collides with the Read EOF convention. When P4 wires the B2F run_exchange driver to Ax25Stream, the driver MUST NOT treat Ok(0) as EOF — loop/retry until session completes, OR change read() to block-until-data-or-closed. Doc comment already on read(). Decide at P4 wiring.

title: [bug] CMS locator transmitted at full precision — ignores position_precision (privacy)
description: Found during the tuxlink-686 position-subsystem brainstorm (2026-05-22). position_precision (default FourCharGrid) is honored for ribbon DISPLAY (formatGridForDisplay) but NOT for the CMS locator transmitted on-air: winlink_backend.rs ~L627 builds locator = config.identity.grid.clone() (full precision), passed to ExchangeConfig.locator and transmitted verbatim by handshake.rs:52 as '; <target> DE <mycall> (<locator>)'. A 6-char stored grid (e.g. a 6-char wizard entry) broadcasts at full precision regardless of the privacy setting. Affects shipping v0.0.1. Fix: reduce the locator to position_precision at construction (a single broadcast_grid() helper). The tuxlink-686 arbiter closes this via broadcast_grid(); decide whether to also patch v0.0.1 standalone before release.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-882-locator-precision. Branch: bd-tuxlink-882/locator-precision. Created 2026-05-22 09:41:17 UTC by basin-arroyo-osprey.

title: Position subsystem: manual grid (inline-edit) + GPS source arbiter (gpsd)
description: v0.1 position subsystem (absorbs tuxlink-2y5 manual-grid + tuxlink-2ob GPS). Brainstormed 2026-05-22 (basin-arroyo-osprey + operator). Single position arbiter holds {source: Manual|Gps, grid, last_fix}. Manual entry via inline-edit in the ribbon (Approach A) + amber MANUAL/GPS source chip. GPS via gpsd client (LC29C already on /dev/ttyAMA0+pps0, gpsd serving). Source contract: explicit source, Manual sticky (GPS never auto-overrides), operator-only switching, precision enforced independent of source (4-char broadcast default), source always visible. Wizard grid = initial value (source defaults Gps); deliberate inline-edit pins Manual. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-22-position-subsystem-design.md.
acceptance_criteria: Spec written + operator-reviewed; impl plan written; manual inline-edit + config_set_grid + source chip + gpsd client + arbiter + Maidenhead conversion + fix-quality gate + config position_source migration; tested via unit + gpsfake NMEA replay (loopback) + live LC29C no-fix path; no live RF.
notes: CHECKPOINT 2026-05-22 maple-yew-opossum: Tasks 5-12 of 12 DONE (config_set_grid+arbiter, CMS locator from arbiter, position_source DTO, GridEdit inline-edit+chip, gpsd TPV parse, gpsd watch+backoff, position_set_source+spawn, gpsfake e2e). Each task got per-task two-stage review (spec+quality) + fixes. Cross-provider Codex gate found 2 P1s, BOTH FIXED in 48187b3: (1) on-air locator now honors gps_state privacy (GPS broadcast only when BroadcastAtPrecision; Off/LocalUiOnly fall back to config grid; gpsd spawn gated on !=Off); (2) position_status now surfaces the live broadcast_grid so the ribbon shows what's transmitted (shared effective_broadcast_locator used by both native_connect + position_status). 212 lib + 41 vitest green; gpsfake e2e validated (JN58td->JN58). All pushed to origin/bd-tuxlink-686/position-subsystem. OWED (operator): browser smoke of GridEdit inline-edit/chip; live LC29C no-fix smoke; decide LocalUiOnly local-display refinement; optional Codex re-confirm of the P1 fix. NOT closed pending operator smokes.

title: Connect-path hardening: bounded DNS, total connect deadline, abort-label accuracy (Codex adrev follow-ups)
description:
Deferred Medium/Low findings from the 2026-05-21 Codex adversarial review of the connect cluster (gqo+9z2). The two High findings (single-flight #1, abort TOCTOU #2) + cleanup #7 were fixed in the cluster before merge; these remain:

#3 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs: to_socket_addrs() DNS resolution is UNBOUNDED before the bounded connect_timeout. A blackholed/hung resolver reintroduces the stall gqo fixed (no socket registered yet, so cms_abort can't unblock it). Fix needs a DNS-with-timeout (threaded resolve + timeout, or async resolver). Low real-world impact now: cms-z + server.winlink.org are single-A and resolve in ~0.07s.

#4 (Med) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: CONNECT_TIMEOUT is per-address, not a total deadline. A host with N blackholed A/AAAA records can block up to N*15s. Fix: pass a total deadline; per-addr remaining = deadline - elapsed. Low impact now (current hosts single-address), but IPv6 AAAA could add addresses.

#5 (Med) winlink_backend.rs abort_aware_outcome: maps ANY error to Cancelled when aborting is set. If a real (pre-abort) failure surfaces just as the operator clicks Abort, it's mislabeled 'CMS connection aborted.' instead of the true reason. Cosmetic but can mask a real error. Fix: track whether abort actually shut down THIS connect's socket (epoch), or only map to Cancelled when a socket was actually torn down.

#6 (Low) winlink/telnet.rs connect_with_timeout: returns only the LAST address error. If addr1 = ConnectionRefused and addr2 = TimedOut, the user sees only TimedOut, losing the more actionable earlier failure. Diagnostic-only.

Raw transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-05-21-connect-cluster-codex.md (local-only, gitignored).
notes: Implemented #3/#4/#6 in telnet.rs (commit c612a48 on bd-tuxlink-lbg/connect-hardening). #5 implemented then REVERTED: Codex re-review (codex review --base main, gpt-5.5) flagged P2 — the only-Cancelled-on-teardown fix regresses the abort contract for pre-socket aborts (Abort during DNS/connect would surface as Error not Cancelled) and didn't fix the socket-exists race anyway. winlink_backend.rs left == main (aborting-keyed mapping is contract-correct). 166 lib green; full suite re-running pre-push. PR pending.

title: Manually set grid/location without GPS (currently fixed, not editable at runtime)
description: The dashboard Grid is read from config and not editable at runtime; there's no GPS yet (tuxlink-2ob hardware-gated). Operators need to set their Maidenhead grid manually (Settings/edit-grid UI) so position is correct without a GPS device. Honors the 4-char default precision (project GPS convention). Distinct from tuxlink-2ob (GPS device integration).
notes: DEFERRED by granite-finch-spruce 2026-05-21 to keep this session focused on shipping the P1 connect cluster (gqo+9z2) to main via the Codex gate + merges. Scoping done: NO runtime Settings screen exists yet (only the wizard sets grid); 2y5 needs (a) a backend config_set_grid command (validate Maidenhead 4/6-char, write_config_atomic, refresh ribbon), (b) a frontend edit-grid surface — UX shape undecided (inline-editable ribbon Grid field vs a new Settings panel), which warrants a brief brainstorm per the project's UI norms. Independent of the connect cluster; clean to do as its own bd-<id> branch next session.

title: Compose window slow to open (second WebKitGTK webview parses full eager bundle)
description: Opening New Message spawns a separate Tauri WebviewWindow = a new WebKitGTK web context that reloads + re-parses the ENTIRE app bundle (App.tsx eagerly imports AppShell/Wizard/mailbox) before routing to <Compose>. On a Pi this is the observed slowness. Operator chose to keep the separate-window architecture (ng3 smoke #8 option a). Fix options: (a) code-split so the /compose route lazy-loads a lean chunk (React.lazy + Suspense on AppShell/Wizard in App.tsx) — keeps option a, cuts the compose webview's parse cost; (b) move compose to an in-window docked panel (no second webview) — fastest, but reverses AMD-6 + compose-window machinery (own brainstorm). Recommend (a) first.

title: Top menu bar + titlebar are native gray; Mock B spec is dark blue (custom chrome)
description: The File/Message/Session menu bar and the window titlebar render in the system gray theme; the approved Mock B design shows a dark-blue top. They are NATIVE (tauri.conf has no decorations:false; menu.rs builds a native Menu via app.set_menu). Matching the spec requires custom window chrome: set decorations:false and build a dark-blue HTML titlebar + menu bar (re-wiring the menu events as HTML, window drag region + min/max/close controls). Reported by operator 2026-05-21.
notes: In progress on bd-tuxlink-0ic/native-winlink-client (worktrees/bd-tuxlink-0ic-native-winlink-client). Spec fd5a5c8 / plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-21-window-chrome-ng3.md. Folds in tuxlink-msr (compose minimal bar) + operator-approved option b (Reply accelerators open a reply window). Subagent-driven execution under build-robust-features.

title: Native mailbox has no read/unread state — messages never mark read on open
description: Clicking a message in the inbox never switches it from unread to read. native_mailbox.rs hardcodes MessageMeta.unread=false (everything appears read) and there is no read-state persistence or mark-read path. Implement: track unread per message in the native store (e.g. a read-marker / per-folder read set), mark read when message_read opens it, and reflect it in mailbox_list so the list + sidebar unread badge update. Reported by operator 2026-05-21.
close_reason: Implemented read/unread state end-to-end: per-message .read sidecars in the native store (inbox-scoped unread), mark_read trait method + NativeBackend override, message_read marks-on-open best-effort, useMessage invalidates mailbox queries for prompt badge refresh. TDD at every layer; full suite green; grim-smoked. Commit b840b4d.

title: Compose opens a separate window with duplicate top-of-pane controls (spec)
description: Clicking New Message opens a wholly separate Tauri window (compose_window.rs / Compose.tsx) that duplicates the main window's top-of-pane controls — wrong per the Mock B spec. Decide the intended model (inline compose pane/modal in the main window, OR a compose window WITHOUT the duplicated main-window chrome) against docs/design and rework. Reported by operator 2026-05-21 during the native-client UI review.

title: Project dashboard: pure-derived bd-first, tailnet-served, single-screen, multi-track + live-session
description: Local-only project dashboard for monitoring tuxlink while out (esp. during long autonomous/parallel agent sessions). Design brainstormed 2026-05-21 (alder-arroyo-bluff); spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-project-dashboard-design.md. Approach A: small Python collector + tiny HTTP server, server-rendered HTML, ~15s recompute cache, served on the tailnet (pandora 100.110.97.120, free port). PURE-DERIVED bd-first: zero hand-maintained progress config — state/blockers/velocity/ETAs computed live from bd + git + gh + worktree mtimes; tracks grouped by bd LABEL (one-time labeling pass; untracked issues bucket, fix-in-bd not in-config). Anti-goal = LFST dashboard drift (config + plan-markdown slug-inference). Trust model: one source per fact, show-your-work drilldown, honest uncertainty (ETA ranges + 'insufficient data'), DRIFT-detection card (merged-not-closed, stalled, unclaimed worktrees), minimal human surface. Scope: 3 tracks (v0.0.1 / native-client / RF-modem) + overall-progress timeline (real date axis, future=range bands) + live-session panel. Read-only; never transmits; systemd user service.
design: Components: collectors/{bd,git,gh,worktrees}.py -> model.py (pure, fixture-testable) -> render.py (Jinja2 self-contained HTML) + server.py (http.server, /api/state.json, cache) + settings.toml (bind/port/repo/refresh only). No DB. Timeline fill=elapsed-time (neutral); work-done shown via pip state + per-track bars. Future milestones=range bands. ETA separates throughput-bound from decision-gated work. Bind tailnet IP not 0.0.0.0. systemctl --user (no sudo). v1 lives in tools/dashboard/ in-repo; extractable later (transfer goal).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-1y0-dashboard. Branch: bd-tuxlink-1y0/dashboard. Created 2026-05-21 16:27:37 UTC by alder-arroyo-bluff.
close_reason: Relocated to standalone repo cameronzucker/tuxlink-project-dashboard (root commit eb4b57f) to avoid contaminating the main tuxlink repo. Design spec + scaffolding live there; the dashboard reads tuxlink via a configured repo path. Pushed branch deleted from tuxlink remote; worktree disposed per ADR 0009.
